  GRPO_ARGS+=(--use-rollout-routing-replay)
fi
if is_true "${USE_OPD}"; then
  GRPO_ARGS+=(
    --use-opd
    --opd-type "${OPD_TYPE}"
    --opd-kl-coef "${OPD_KL_COEF}"
  )
  if [[ -n "${OPD_TEACHER_LOAD}" ]]; then
    GRPO_ARGS+=(--opd-teacher-load "${OPD_TEACHER_LOAD}")
  fi
  if [[ -n "${OPD_TEACHER_CKPT_STEP}" ]]; then
    GRPO_ARGS+=(--opd-teacher-ckpt-step "${OPD_TEACHER_CKPT_STEP}")
  fi
fi
if is_true "${USE_OPSM}"; then
  GRPO_ARGS+=(
    --use-opsm
    --opsm-delta "${OPSM_DELTA}"
  )
fi

OPTIMIZER_ARGS=(
  --optimizer "${OPTIMIZER}"
  --lr "${LR}"
  --lr-decay-style "${LR_DECAY_STYLE}"
  --weight-decay "${WEIGHT_DECAY}"
  --adam-beta1 "${ADAM_BETA1}"
  --adam-beta2 "${ADAM_BETA2}"
  --clip-grad "${CLIP_GRAD}"
)

SGLANG_ARGS=(
  --rollout-num-gpus-per-engine "${ROLLOUT_NUM_GPUS_PER_ENGINE}"
  --sglang-mem-fraction-static "${SGLANG_MEM_FRAC}"
)

MISC_ARGS=(
  --attention-dropout 0.0
  --hidden-dropout 0.0
  --accumulate-allreduce-grads-in-fp32
  --attention-softmax-in-fp32
  --attention-backend flash
  --actor-num-nodes "${ACTOR_NUM_NODES}"
  --actor-num-gpus-per-node "${ACTOR_NUM_GPUS_PER_NODE}"
  --rollout-num-gpus "${ROLLOUT_NUM_GPUS}"
  --colocate
  --offload-rollout
  --megatron-to-hf-mode bridge
)
if [[ -n "${CRITIC_NUM_NODES}" ]]; then
  MISC_ARGS+=(--critic-num-nodes "${CRITIC_NUM_NODES}")
fi
if [[ -n "${CRITIC_NUM_GPUS_PER_NODE}" ]]; then
  MISC_ARGS+=(--critic-num-gpus-per-node "${CRITIC_NUM_GPUS_PER_NODE}")
fi

WANDB_ARGS=(
  --use-wandb
  --wandb-mode "${WANDB_MODE}"
  --wandb-project "${WANDB_PROJECT}"
  --wandb-group "${WANDB_GROUP}"
  --wandb-dir "${WANDB_DIR}"
  --disable-wandb-random-suffix
)
if [[ -n "${WANDB_API_KEY}" ]]; then
  WANDB_ARGS+=(--wandb-key "${WANDB_API_KEY}")
fi

CUSTOM_ARGS=(
  --custom-generate-function-path generate_with_search.generate
  --custom-rm-path generate_with_search.reward_func
)

RUNTIME_ENV_JSON="$(cat <<EOF
{"env_vars":{"PYTHONPATH":"${MEGATRON_DIR}:${SEARCH_R1_DIR}","CUDA_DEVICE_MAX_CONNECTIONS":"1","MASTER_ADDR":"127.0.0.1","SEARCH_R1_SEARCH_BACKEND":"${SEARCH_BACKEND}","SEARCH_R1_LOCAL_SEARCH_URL":"${LOCAL_SEARCH_URL}","SEARCH_R1_LOCAL_PROXY":"${LOCAL_SEARCH_PROXY}","SEARCH_R1_SERPER_API_KEY":"${SEARCH_R1_SERPER_API_KEY}","SEARCH_R1_RETURN_LOGPROB":"${SEARCH_RETURN_LOGPROB}","SEARCH_R1_SEARCH_CONCURRENCY":"${SEARCH_CONCURRENCY}","SEARCH_R1_MAX_TURNS":"${SEARCH_MAX_TURNS}","SEARCH_R1_TOPK":"${SEARCH_TOPK}","SEARCH_R1_GOOGLE_SNIPPET_ONLY":"${GOOGLE_SNIPPET_ONLY}","SEARCH_R1_GOOGLE_MIN_INTERVAL":"${GOOGLE_MIN_INTERVAL}","SEARCH_R1_GOOGLE_MAX_RETRIES":"${GOOGLE_MAX_RETRIES}","SEARCH_R1_GOOGLE_BACKOFF_BASE":"${GOOGLE_BACKOFF_BASE}","SEARCH_R1_GOOGLE_BACKOFF_CAP":"${GOOGLE_BACKOFF_CAP}","no_proxy":"127.0.0.1,localhost","NO_PROXY":"127.0.0.1,localhost","http_proxy":"http://127.0.0.1:7898","https_proxy":"http://127.0.0.1:7898","all_proxy":"http://127.0.0.1:7898"}}
EOF
)"

set -x
ray job submit --address="http://127.0.0.1:8265" \
  --runtime-env-json="${RUNTIME_ENV_JSON}" \
  -- python3 train.py \
  "${MODEL_ARGS[@]}" \
  "${CKPT_ARGS[@]}" \
  "${ROLLOUT_ARGS[@]}" \
  "${OPTIMIZER_ARGS[@]}" \
  "${GRPO_ARGS[@]}" \
  "${PERF_ARGS[@]}" \
  "${EVAL_ARGS[@]}" \
  "${SGLANG_ARGS[@]}" \
  "${MISC_ARGS[@]}" \
  "${WANDB_ARGS[@]}" \
  "${CUSTOM_ARGS[@]}" | tee "${LOG_DIR}/train_console.log"

set +x
if [[ "${EXPORT_TEST_TRAJ}" == "1" ]]; then
  if ! [[ "${TEST_EXPORT_EVERY_N_EVAL}" =~ ^[1-9][0-9]*$ ]]; then
    echo "WARN: TEST_EXPORT_EVERY_N_EVAL=${TEST_EXPORT_EVERY_N_EVAL} is invalid, fallback to 1"
    TEST_EXPORT_EVERY_N_EVAL=1
  fi

  eval_dir="${DEBUG_DIR}/rollout_data"
  if compgen -G "${eval_dir}/eval_*.pt" > /dev/null; then
    mapfile -t eval_pts < <(find "${eval_dir}" -maxdepth 1 -type f -name 'eval_*.pt' | sort -V)
    if [[ "${TEST_EXPORT_MODE}" == "final" ]]; then
      last_idx=$((${#eval_pts[@]} - 1))
      eval_pts=("${eval_pts[${last_idx}]}")
    elif [[ "${TEST_EXPORT_MODE}" != "all" ]]; then
      echo "WARN: TEST_EXPORT_MODE=${TEST_EXPORT_MODE} is unsupported, fallback to final"
      last_idx=$((${#eval_pts[@]} - 1))
      eval_pts=("${eval_pts[${last_idx}]}")
    fi

    export_idx=0
    for eval_pt in "${eval_pts[@]}"; do
      export_idx=$((export_idx + 1))
      if (((export_idx - 1) % TEST_EXPORT_EVERY_N_EVAL != 0)); then
        continue
      fi
      bn="$(basename "${eval_pt}")"
      rid="${bn#eval_}"
      rid="${rid%.pt}"
      if [[ "${rid}" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]]; then
        step_id=$((rid + 1))
      else
        step_id="${rid}"
      fi

      out_jsonl="${RUN_ROOT}/test_${step_id}.jsonl"
      out_metrics="${RUN_ROOT}/test_${step_id}_metrics.json"
      echo "Exporting eval trajectories from ${eval_pt} -> ${out_jsonl}"
      python3 "${SEARCH_R1_DIR}/export_test_traj.py" \
        --eval-rollout-pt "${eval_pt}" \
        --output-jsonl "${out_jsonl}" \
        --output-metrics-json "${out_metrics}" | tee -a "${LOG_DIR}/test_export.log"
    done
  else
    echo "No eval_*.pt found under ${eval_dir}, skip test trajectory export."
  fi
fi
#!/usr/bin/env bash

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" &>/dev/null && pwd)"
DEFAULT_CONFIG_FILE="${SCRIPT_DIR}/configs/ppo_train.yaml"

# PPO й»ҳи®Өе…ҘеҸЈпјҡд»ҚеӨҚз”ЁеҺҹе§Ӣ Search-R1 rollout дёҺе·Ҙе…·и°ғз”ЁйҖ»иҫ‘гҖӮ
export CONFIG_FILE="${CONFIG_FILE:-${DEFAULT_CONFIG_FILE}}"
export ADVANTAGE_ESTIMATOR="${ADVANTAGE_ESTIMATOR:-ppo}"

# critic зӣёе…ій»ҳи®ӨеҖјпјҲеҸҜеңЁ YAML иҰҶзӣ–пјү
export NUM_CRITIC_ONLY_STEPS="${NUM_CRITIC_ONLY_STEPS:-0}"
export VALUE_CLIP="${VALUE_CLIP:-0.2}"

if [[ -z "${CRITIC_SAVE:-}" ]]; then
  ts="$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S)"
  export EXP_NAME="${EXP_NAME:-search-r1-ppo-google-${ts}}"
  # д»…еңЁжңӘжҳҫејҸи®ҫзҪ® CRITIC_SAVE ж—¶пјҢз»ҷдёҖдёӘзӢ¬з«Ӣзӣ®еҪ•пјҢйҒҝе…ҚдёҺ actor ckpt ж··ж·ҶгҖӮ
  export CRITIC_SAVE="${SCRIPT_DIR}/train_log/${EXP_NAME}/ckpt_critic"
fi

exec "${SCRIPT_DIR}/run_qwen2.5_3B_google_grpo_jkh.sh" "$@"
# Search-R1 е…ЁйҮҸи®ӯз»ғй…ҚзҪ®пјҲGoogle Search + GRPOпјү
# еҗҜеҠЁж–№ејҸпјҡ
#   CONFIG_FILE=/mnt/workspace/jkh/slime/examples/search-r1/configs/full_train.yaml \
#   bash /mnt/workspace/jkh/slime/examples/search-r1/run_qwen2.5_3B_google_grpo_jkh.sh
#
# жӯҘж•°и§„еҲҷпјҡ
# 1) иӢҘ NUM_EPOCH йқһз©әпјҢи„ҡжң¬дј  --num-epochпјҢеҝҪз•Ҙ NUM_ROLLOUTгҖӮ
# 2) еҗҰеҲҷдј  --num-rollout=NUM_ROLLOUTгҖӮ
# 3) зәҰ 1 дёӘ epoch зҡ„ step ж•° вүҲ ж•°жҚ®жқЎж•° / ROLLOUT_BATCH_SIZEгҖӮ
#
# дҝқеӯҳ/иҜ„дј°и§„еҲҷпјҡ
# - SAVE_INTERVALпјҡжҜҸйҡ”еӨҡе°‘дёӘ rollout step еӯҳ ckptгҖӮ
# - EVAL_INTERVALпјҡжҜҸйҡ”еӨҡе°‘дёӘ rollout step и·‘ evalгҖӮ
# - EXPORT_TEST_TRAJ + TEST_EXPORT_*пјҡжҺ§еҲ¶еҜјеҮә test_xxx.jsonl зҡ„йў‘зҺҮгҖӮ

# ===== еҝ…еЎ«еҜҶй’Ҙ =====
# Google жҗңзҙўпјҲSerperпјүAPI keyпјӣдёҚеҪұе“ҚжҳҫеӯҳпјҢдҪҶдјҡеҪұе“ҚжҗңзҙўеҸҜз”ЁжҖ§дёҺи®ӯз»ғзЁіе®ҡжҖ§гҖӮ
SEARCH_R1_SERPER_API_KEY: "0ec804b592087df08dc3a0d98ac5cbb67ad549db"

# ===== еҸҜйҖүпјҡW&B =====
# еҸҜйҖүпјҡд№ҹеҸҜз”Ёжң¬жңә wandb loginпјҢдёҚеҶҷиҝҷйҮҢгҖӮ
# WANDB_KEY: "e5780f752ed310ff6307"
WANDB_PROJECT: "search-r1"

# ===== еҸҜйҖүпјҡе®һйӘҢеҗҚ =====
# дёҚеЎ«еҲҷи„ҡжң¬иҮӘеҠЁдҪҝз”Ёж—¶й—ҙжҲіеҗҺзјҖгҖӮ
# EXP_NAME: "search-r1-full-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S)"

# ===== и·Ҝеҫ„ =====
# е·ҘдҪңзӣ®еҪ•ж №и·Ҝеҫ„гҖӮ
BASE_DIR: "/mnt/workspace/jkh"
# slime д»“еә“и·Ҝеҫ„гҖӮ
SLIME_DIR: "/mnt/workspace/jkh/slime"
# Megatron-LM и·Ҝеҫ„гҖӮ
MEGATRON_DIR: "/mnt/workspace/jkh/Megatron-LM"
# еҲқе§ӢжЁЎеһӢи·Ҝеҫ„пјҲHF ж јејҸпјүгҖӮ
MODEL_DIR: "/mnt/workspace/jkh/model/Qwen3-4B"
# search-r1 зӨәдҫӢи·Ҝеҫ„гҖӮ
SEARCH_R1_DIR: "/mnt/workspace/jkh/slime/examples/search-r1"
# ж•°жҚ®зӣ®еҪ•гҖӮ
DATA_DIR: "/mnt/workspace/jkh/slime/examples/search-r1/data"
# и®ӯз»ғйӣҶи·Ҝеҫ„пјҲ97%пјүгҖӮ
TRAIN_PARQUET: "/mnt/workspace/jkh/slime/examples/search-r1/data/train_97.parquet"
# жөӢиҜ•йӣҶи·Ҝеҫ„пјҲ3%пјүгҖӮ
TEST_PARQUET: "/mnt/workspace/jkh/slime/examples/search-r1/data/test_3.parquet"

# ===== GPU / жҳҫеӯҳзӣёе…і =====
# и®ӯз»ғеҸҜи§Ғ GPU ж•°гҖӮ8xA100-80G з©әй—Іж—¶е»әи®®жӢүж»ЎгҖӮ
NUM_GPUS: 8
# rollout дҪҝз”Ёзҡ„ GPU ж•°гҖӮдёҺи®ӯз»ғеҗҢй…Қ 8пјҢжҸҗй«ҳ rollout еҗһеҗҗгҖӮ
ROLLOUT_NUM_GPUS: 8
# жҜҸдёӘ rollout engine еҚ з”Ёзҡ„ GPU ж•°гҖӮеўһеӨ§еҸҜйҷҚдҪҺеҚ•еҚЎеҺӢеҠӣпјҢдҪҶдјҡеҮҸе°‘е№¶иЎҢ engine ж•°гҖӮ
ROLLOUT_NUM_GPUS_PER_ENGINE: 1
# и®ӯз»ғ TP еӨ§е°ҸгҖӮ3B жЁЎеһӢз”Ё TP=1 еҸҜеҮҸе°‘йҖҡдҝЎе№¶жҸҗеҚҮйҖҹеәҰгҖӮ
TENSOR_MODEL_PARALLEL_SIZE: 1
# иҮӘеҠЁйҖүеҚЎж—¶зҡ„жңҖе°Ҹз©әй—ІжҳҫеӯҳйҳҲеҖјпјҲMBпјүгҖӮз©әй—ІйӣҶзҫӨдёӢжҸҗй«ҳйҳҲеҖјжӣҙзЁігҖӮ
MIN_FREE_MEM_MB: 60000
# еҠЁжҖҒ batch дёӢжҜҸеҚЎжңҖеӨ§ token дёҠйҷҗгҖӮ8x80G дёӢ 6144 йҖҡеёёе…јйЎҫйҖҹеәҰдёҺ OOM е®үе…ЁгҖӮ
MAX_TOKENS_PER_GPU: 6144
# rollout(sglang) йқҷжҖҒжҳҫеӯҳеҚ жҜ”гҖӮжҸҗй«ҳеҲ° 0.32 жҸҗеҚҮжҺЁзҗҶеҗһеҗҗпјҢеҗҢж—¶дҝқз•ҷе®үе…ЁдҪҷйҮҸгҖӮ
SGLANG_MEM_FRAC: 0.32

# ===== и®ӯз»ғжӯҘж•° / йҮҮж ·ж ёеҝғеҸӮж•° =====
# еӣәе®ҡ rollout жӯҘж•°пјҲд»…еңЁ NUM_EPOCH дёәз©әж—¶з”ҹж•ҲпјүгҖӮиҝҷйҮҢдҪңдёәеҗҺеӨҮеҖјгҖӮ
NUM_ROLLOUT: 1200
# жҢү epoch жҺ§еҲ¶и®ӯз»ғж—¶дҪҝз”Ёпјӣи®ҫдёә 1 иЎЁзӨәе®Ңж•ҙ 1 дёӘ epochпјҢйҖҡеёёжӣҙзЁігҖӮ
NUM_EPOCH: 1
# жҜҸдёӘ rollout step йҮҮеӨҡе°‘дёӘ promptгҖӮ
# и¶ҠеӨ§пјҡеҚ• step жӣҙж…ўгҖҒж ·жң¬жӣҙеӨҡпјӣж•ҙдҪ“жҳҫеӯҳз•ҘеўһгҖӮ8 еҚЎдёӢи®ҫ 8пјҢж•ҲзҺҮе’ҢзЁіе®ҡжҖ§жӣҙе№іиЎЎгҖӮ
ROLLOUT_BATCH_SIZE: 8
# жҜҸдёӘ prompt йҮҮж ·еҮ жқЎеҖҷйҖүпјҲGRPO еҲҶз»„еӨ§е°ҸпјүгҖӮ
# и¶ҠеӨ§пјҡеҘ–еҠұжҺ’еәҸдҝЎеҸ·жӣҙзЁіпјҢдҪҶ token ејҖй”Җе’Ңж—¶й•ҝжҳҺжҳҫеўһеҠ гҖӮ
N_SAMPLES_PER_PROMPT: 4
# PPO/GRPO жӣҙж–°ж—¶зҡ„ global batchгҖӮи®ҫдёә 32пјҲ8*4пјүпјҢжўҜеәҰз»ҹи®ЎжӣҙзЁігҖӮ
GLOBAL_BATCH_SIZE: 32
# rollout жңҖеӨ§з”ҹжҲҗй•ҝеәҰгҖӮ512 еҸҜжҳҺжҳҫйҷҚдҪҺжҲӘж–ӯзҺҮпјҢеҗҢж—¶йҒҝе…Қ 1000 еёҰжқҘзҡ„й«ҳејҖй”ҖгҖӮ
ROLLOUT_MAX_RESPONSE_LEN: 512
# rollout йҮҮж ·жё©еәҰгҖӮ1.0 дҝқжҢҒжҺўзҙўпјҢй…ҚеҗҲзҶө/KL зәҰжқҹжӣҙеҲ©дәҺжҢҒз»ӯжҸҗеҚҮгҖӮ
ROLLOUT_TEMPERATURE: 1.0

# ===== иҜ„дј° / дҝқеӯҳйў‘зҺҮ =====
# жҜҸйҡ”еӨҡе°‘ step дҝқеӯҳдёҖж¬Ў checkpointгҖӮ
SAVE_INTERVAL: 100
# жҜҸйҡ”еӨҡе°‘ step еҒҡдёҖж¬ЎиҜ„дј°пјҲдјҡйўқеӨ–иҖ—ж—¶пјүгҖӮ25 жӣҙеҲ©дәҺи§ӮеҜҹ reward/acc и¶ӢеҠҝгҖӮ
EVAL_INTERVAL: 25
# 1=и·іиҝҮи®ӯз»ғеүҚзҡ„ step0 иҜ„дј°пјӣеҸҜеҮҸе°‘еҶ·еҗҜеҠЁејҖй”ҖгҖӮ
SKIP_EVAL_BEFORE_TRAIN: 1
# жҜҸдёӘиҜ„дј°ж ·жң¬з”ҹжҲҗеҮ жқЎзӯ”жЎҲпјҲйҖҡеёё 1 еҚіеҸҜпјүгҖӮ
N_SAMPLES_PER_EVAL_PROMPT: 1
# eval top-kпјҲйҖҡеёё 1пјүгҖӮ
EVAL_TOP_K: 1

# ===== GRPO / PPO / KL е…ій”®еҸӮж•° =====
# дјҳеҠҝдј°и®ЎеҷЁгҖӮSearch-R1 йҖҡеёёз”Ё grpoгҖӮ
ADVANTAGE_ESTIMATOR: "grpo"
# зҶөжӯЈеҲҷзі»ж•°гҖӮе°Ҹе№…ејҖеҗҜжҺўзҙўпјҢйҒҝе…Қзӯ–з•ҘиҝҮж—©еЎҢзј©гҖӮ
ENTROPY_COEF: 0.001
# PPO clip дёӢз•ҢпјҲж ёеҝғзЁіе®ҡеҸӮж•°пјүгҖӮ
EPS_CLIP: 0.2
# PPO clip дёҠз•ҢпјҲеҸҜдёҚеҜ№з§°и®ҫзҪ®пјүгҖӮи®ҫеӨ§дёҖдәӣйҖҡеёёжӣҙжҝҖиҝӣгҖӮ
EPS_CLIP_HIGH: 0.28
# еҘ–еҠұж•ҙеҪў KL зі»ж•°пјҲreward shaping и·Ҝеҫ„пјүгҖӮи®ҫдёә 0.02 жҠ‘еҲ¶зӯ–з•ҘжјӮз§»гҖӮ
KL_COEF: 0.02
# жҳҜеҗҰеҗҜз”Ё KL loss йЎ№пјҲloss и·Ҝеҫ„пјүгҖӮе…ій—ӯпјҢйҒҝе…ҚдёҺ KL_COEF еҸ еҠ еҶІзӘҒгҖӮ
USE_KL_LOSS: 0
# KL loss зі»ж•°пјҲд»… USE_KL_LOSS=1 ж—¶з”ҹж•ҲпјүгҖӮе…ій—ӯ KL loss ж—¶дҝқжҢҒ 0гҖӮ
KL_LOSS_COEF: 0.0
# KL loss еҪўејҸпјҡk1/k2/k3/low_var_klгҖӮ
KL_LOSS_TYPE: "k1"
# жҳҜеҗҰеҗҜз”Ё unbiased KL дј°и®ЎгҖӮ
USE_UNBIASED_KL: 0
# GAE gammaпјҲGRPO дёӢйҖҡеёёдҝқжҢҒ 1.0пјүгҖӮ
GAMMA: 1.0
# GAE lambdaпјҲGRPO дёӢйҖҡеёёдҝқжҢҒ 1.0пјүгҖӮ
LAMBDA: 1.0
# жҳҜеҗҰеҪ’дёҖеҢ– advantageпјҲ1 ејҖпјҢ0 е…іпјүгҖӮејҖеҗҜеҸҜжҸҗеҚҮи®ӯз»ғзЁіе®ҡжҖ§гҖӮ
NORMALIZE_ADVANTAGES: 1
# GRPO ж ҮеҮҶе·®еҪ’дёҖеҢ–пјҲ1 ејҖпјҢ0 е…іпјүгҖӮ
# е…іжҺүеҗҺи®ӯз»ғдҝЎеҸ·еҸҜиғҪжӣҙвҖңзЎ¬вҖқпјҢдҪҶжіўеҠЁйҖҡеёёжӣҙеӨ§гҖӮ
GRPO_STD_NORMALIZATION: 1
# reward еҪ’дёҖеҢ–пјҲ1 ејҖпјҢ0 е…іпјүгҖӮ
REWARDS_NORMALIZATION: 1
# жҳҜеҗҰдҪҝз”Ё rollout ж—¶иҝ”еӣһзҡ„ logprob еҒҡ IS жҜ”зҺҮгҖӮ
USE_ROLLOUT_LOGPROBS: 0
# жҳҜеҗҰжҜҸдёӘ rollout еҗҺйҮҚзҪ®дјҳеҢ–еҷЁзҠ¶жҖҒпјҲзЁіе®ҡжҖ§е®һйӘҢеҸӮж•°пјҢйҖҡеёёе…ій—ӯпјүгҖӮ
RESET_OPTIMIZER_STATES: 0
# жҳҜеҗҰеҗҜз”Ё TISпјҲзҰ»зӯ–з•Ҙдҝ®жӯЈпјүпјӣејҖеҗҜеҗҺеўһеҠ и®Ўз®—еӨҚжқӮеәҰгҖӮ
USE_TIS: 0
# TIS дёҠз•ҢиЈҒеүӘгҖӮ
TIS_CLIP: 2.0
# TIS дёӢз•ҢиЈҒеүӘгҖӮ
TIS_CLIP_LOW: 0.0

# ===== дјҳеҢ–еҷЁеҸӮж•° =====
# дјҳеҢ–еҷЁзұ»еһӢпјҲеҪ“еүҚи„ҡжң¬й»ҳи®Ө adamпјүгҖӮ
OPTIMIZER: "adam"
# еӯҰд№ зҺҮгҖӮи¶ҠеӨ§ж”¶ж•ӣжӣҙеҝ«дҪҶдёҚзЁіе®ҡйЈҺйҷ©жӣҙй«ҳгҖӮ
LR: 1.0e-6
# еӯҰд№ зҺҮиЎ°еҮҸзӯ–з•ҘпјҲеёёз”Ё constantпјүгҖӮ
LR_DECAY_STYLE: "constant"
# жқғйҮҚиЎ°еҮҸгҖӮ
WEIGHT_DECAY: 0.01
# Adam beta1гҖӮ
ADAM_BETA1: 0.9
# Adam beta2гҖӮ
ADAM_BETA2: 0.98
# жўҜеәҰиЈҒеүӘдёҠйҷҗгҖӮи¶Ҡе°Ҹи¶ҠзЁідҪҶеҸҜиғҪжҠ‘еҲ¶жңүж•Ҳжӣҙж–°гҖӮ
CLIP_GRAD: 1.0

# ===== жҗңзҙўиЎҢдёә / йҳІжӯў Too Many Requests(429) =====
# е№¶еҸ‘жҗңзҙўж•°пјҲжҜҸдёӘиҝӣзЁӢпјүгҖӮ8 еҚЎдёӢжҸҗй«ҳеҲ° 4пјҢе…јйЎҫеҗһеҗҗдёҺйҷҗжөҒйЈҺйҷ©гҖӮ
SEARCH_CONCURRENCY: 4
# жҜҸжқЎж ·жң¬жңҖеӨҡе·Ҙе…·и°ғз”ЁиҪ®ж¬ЎгҖӮи¶ҠеӨ§ж•ҲжһңжҪңеҠӣжӣҙй«ҳпјҢдҪҶйҖҹеәҰжӣҙж…ўгҖҒtoken жӣҙеӨҡгҖӮ
SEARCH_MAX_TURNS: 2
# жҜҸж¬Ў search иҝ”еӣһ top-k ж–ҮжЎЈж•°гҖӮ4 жҜ” 3 дҝЎжҒҜжӣҙе……еҲҶпјҢйҖҡеёёжңүеҲ©дәҺ reward/accгҖӮ
SEARCH_TOPK: 4
# 1=еҸӘз”Ё snippetпјҲжӣҙеҝ«жӣҙзЁіпјүпјӣ0=жҠ“зҪ‘йЎөжӯЈж–ҮпјҲжӣҙж…ўпјҢеӨұиҙҘйқўжӣҙеӨ§пјүгҖӮ
GOOGLE_SNIPPET_ONLY: 1
# дёӨж¬ЎдёҠжёё Google иҜ·жұӮжңҖе°Ҹй—ҙйҡ”з§’ж•°пјӣеңЁе№¶еҸ‘жҸҗй«ҳеҗҺи®ҫдёә 0.8 е…јйЎҫеҗһеҗҗдёҺзЁіжҖҒгҖӮ
GOOGLE_MIN_INTERVAL: 0.8
# йҒҮеҲ° 429/5xx зҡ„жңҖеӨ§йҮҚиҜ•ж¬Ўж•°гҖӮ
GOOGLE_MAX_RETRIES: 12
# жҢҮж•°йҖҖйҒҝеә•ж•°пјҲйҮҚиҜ•зӯүеҫ…йҖ’еўһйҖҹеәҰпјүгҖӮ
GOOGLE_BACKOFF_BASE: 1.5
# жҢҮж•°йҖҖйҒҝжңҖеӨ§зӯүеҫ…дёҠйҷҗпјҲз§’пјүгҖӮ
GOOGLE_BACKOFF_CAP: 30
# жҳҜеҗҰи®© rollout иҝ”еӣһ token logprobпјҲз”ЁдәҺйғЁеҲҶ GRPO/TIS жҢҮж ҮпјүгҖӮ
SEARCH_RETURN_LOGPROB: 1

# ===== жөӢиҜ•иҪЁиҝ№еҜјеҮә =====
# 1=и®ӯз»ғеҗҺиҮӘеҠЁеҜјеҮә test_xxx.jsonlпјӣ0=дёҚеҜјеҮәгҖӮ
EXPORT_TEST_TRAJ: 1
# final=еҸӘеҜјеҮәжңҖеҗҺдёҖж¬Ў evalпјӣall=еҜјеҮәжүҖжңү eval зӮ№гҖӮ
TEST_EXPORT_MODE: "all"
# TEST_EXPORT_MODE=all ж—¶пјҢжҜҸйҡ”еӨҡе°‘дёӘ eval зӮ№еҜјеҮәдёҖж¬ЎгҖӮ
TEST_EXPORT_EVERY_N_EVAL: 1

# ===== зҪ‘з»ң / д»ЈзҗҶ =====
# д»ЈзҗҶзҺҜеўғеҸҳйҮҸеҲ·ж–°й—ҙйҡ”пјҲз§’пјүпјҢз”ЁдәҺй•ҝд»»еҠЎзҪ‘з»ңзЁіе®ҡгҖӮ
PROXY_REFRESH_SECS: 300

# GAEгҖӮ

LAMBDA: 1.0
# advantage ж ҮеҮҶеҢ–гҖӮ

# еҜ№ GRPO з”ҹж•ҲпјӣPPO дҝқз•ҷиҜҘйЎ№д»…дёәз»ҹдёҖй…ҚзҪ®жҺҘеҸЈгҖӮ
GRPO_STD_NORMALIZATION: 1
# reward ж ҮеҮҶеҢ–пјҲPPO д№ҹеҸҜз”ЁпјүгҖӮ

# ===== еҸӮиҖғжЁЎеһӢ / йўқеӨ–зЁіе®ҡйЎ№ =====
# ref жЁЎеһӢжӣҙж–°й—ҙйҡ”пјҲrollout stepпјүпјҢз•ҷз©әиЎЁзӨәдёҚжӣҙж–° refгҖӮ
REF_UPDATE_INTERVAL: ""
# жҢҮе®ҡ ref checkpoint зҡ„ stepпјҲдёҖиҲ¬з•ҷз©әпјүгҖӮ
REF_CKPT_STEP: ""
# жҳҜеҗҰдҪҝз”Ё rollout дҫ§ logprob еҒҡ IS жҜ”зҺҮгҖӮ
USE_ROLLOUT_LOGPROBS: 0
# жҳҜеҗҰи®Ўз®— rollout entropyпјҲз”ЁдәҺйғЁеҲҶзү№ж®Ҡ mask/еҲҶжһҗпјүгҖӮ
USE_ROLLOUT_ENTROPY: 0
# жҳҜеҗҰжҜҸдёӘ rollout йҮҚзҪ®дјҳеҢ–еҷЁзҠ¶жҖҒгҖӮ
RESET_OPTIMIZER_STATES: 0

# ===== TIS / mismatch / OPSMпјҲеҸҜйҖүй«ҳзә§еҠҹиғҪпјү =====
USE_TIS: 0
TIS_CLIP: 2.0
TIS_CLIP_LOW: 0.0
GET_MISMATCH_METRICS: 0
CUSTOM_TIS_FUNCTION_PATH: ""
CUSTOM_PG_LOSS_REDUCER_FUNCTION_PATH: ""
USE_ROUTING_REPLAY: 0
USE_ROLLOUT_ROUTING_REPLAY: 0
USE_OPSM: 0
OPSM_DELTA: 1.0e-4

# ===== OPDпјҲеҸҜйҖүпјҢдёҺ PPO еҸҜеҸ еҠ пјү =====
# ејҖеҗҜеҗҺдјҡеңЁдјҳеҠҝдёҠеҸ еҠ и’ёйҰҸ KL йЎ№гҖӮ
USE_OPD: 0
# еҸҜйҖү: sglang / megatronпјӣд»… USE_OPD=1 ж—¶з”ҹж•ҲгҖӮ
OPD_TYPE: ""
OPD_KL_COEF: 1.0
# д»… OPD_TYPE=megatron ж—¶йңҖиҰҒ teacher ckptгҖӮ
OPD_TEACHER_LOAD: ""
OPD_TEACHER_CKPT_STEP: ""

# ===== дјҳеҢ–еҷЁ =====
OPTIMIZER: "adam"
LR: 1.0e-6
LR_DECAY_STYLE: "constant"
WEIGHT_DECAY: 0.01
ADAM_BETA1: 0.9
ADAM_BETA2: 0.98
CLIP_GRAD: 1.0

# ===== жҗңзҙўеҗҺз«ҜпјҲдёҺ GRPO зӣёеҗҢ rollout е·Ҙе…·и°ғз”Ёпјү =====
SEARCH_BACKEND: "google"
LOCAL_SEARCH_URL: "http://127.0.0.1:8000/retrieve"
LOCAL_SEARCH_PROXY: ""

SEARCH_CONCURRENCY: 4
SEARCH_MAX_TURNS: 2
SEARCH_TOPK: 4
GOOGLE_SNIPPET_ONLY: 1
GOOGLE_MIN_INTERVAL: 0.8
GOOGLE_MAX_RETRIES: 12
GOOGLE_BACKOFF_BASE: 1.5
GOOGLE_BACKOFF_CAP: 30
SEARCH_RETURN_LOGPROB: 1

# ===== еҜјеҮә =====
EXPORT_TEST_TRAJ: 1
TEST_EXPORT_MODE: "all"
TEST_EXPORT_EVERY_N_EVAL: 1

# ===== зҪ‘з»ң =====
PROXY_REFRESH_SECS: 300
# иҜҙжҳҺпјҡ
# 1) иҝҷжҳҜгҖҢW&B жҢҮж Үйҳ…иҜ»жҢҮеҚ—гҖҚй…ҚзҪ®ж–Үд»¶пјҢдё»иҰҒз»ҷдәәзңӢпјҢдёҚдјҡзӣҙжҺҘж”№еҸҳи®ӯз»ғйҖ»иҫ‘гҖӮ
# 2) дҪ еҸҜд»ҘжҠҠиҝҷйҮҢзҡ„ metric еҗҚз§°зӣҙжҺҘеӨҚеҲ¶еҲ° W&B зҡ„еӣҫиЎЁжҲ–жҗңзҙўжЎҶдёӯгҖӮ
# 3) жң¬йЎ№зӣ®дёӯпјҢreward дёҚеңЁ train/* дёӢпјҢиҖҢдё»иҰҒеңЁ rollout/* е’Ң eval/* дёӢгҖӮ

version: 1
project: "search-r1"

# дёүзұ»жҢҮж Үзҡ„жЁӘиҪҙжҳҜеҲҶејҖзҡ„пјҢе…ҲзңӢжё… x иҪҙеҶҚи§ЈиҜ»жӣІзәҝпјҡ
# - train/*   еҜ№еә” train/stepпјҲи®ӯз»ғжӣҙж–°жӯҘпјү
# - rollout/* еҜ№еә” rollout/stepпјҲжҜҸж¬Ў rolloutпјү
# - eval/*    еҜ№еә” eval/stepпјҲи§ҰеҸ‘иҜ„дј°ж—¶жүҚжңүзӮ№пјү
step_axes:
  train_step: "train/step"
  rollout_step: "rollout/step"
  eval_step: "eval/step"

# дҪ жңҖе…іеҝғзҡ„гҖҢreward / loss / зҶө / KLгҖҚеҜ№еә”еҗҚз§°еҰӮдёӢгҖӮ
# жіЁж„ҸпјҡеҰӮжһң W&B йҮҢжҗңдёҚеҲ° rewardпјҢдјҳе…Ҳжҗң rollout/raw_reward е’Ң eval/search_r1_testгҖӮ
core_metrics:
  reward_train_raw: "rollout/raw_reward"
  reward_train_used_for_update: "rollout/rewards"
  reward_eval: "eval/search_r1_test"
  loss_total: "train/loss"
  loss_pg: "train/pg_loss"
  entropy_proxy: "train/entropy_loss"
  kl_ppo: "train/ppo_kl"
  kl_loss_optional: "train/kl_loss"

# жң¬е®һйӘҢпјҲsearch-r1пјүеёёи§ҒжҢҮж ҮпјҲеҸҜзӣҙжҺҘеңЁ W&B ж·»еҠ еӣҫиЎЁпјүгҖӮ
# жҸҗзӨәпјҡдёҚеҗҢй…ҚзҪ®дёӢеҸҜиғҪдјҡеӨҡ/е°‘дёҖдәӣжҢҮж ҮпјҢиҝҷжҳҜжӯЈеёёзҡ„гҖӮ
recommended_panels:
  train:
    - "train/loss"
    - "train/pg_loss"
    - "train/entropy_loss"
    - "train/ppo_kl"
    - "train/pg_clipfrac"
    - "train/grad_norm"
    - "train/train_rollout_logprob_abs_diff"
  rollout:
    - "rollout/raw_reward"
    - "rollout/rewards"
    - "rollout/response_lengths"
    - "rollout/truncated"
    - "rollout/log_probs"
    - "rollout/rollout_log_probs"
    - "rollout/advantages"
    - "rollout/returns"
  eval:
    - "eval/search_r1_test"
    - "eval/search_r1_test/response_len/mean"
    - "eval/search_r1_test/response_len/max"
    - "eval/search_r1_test/repetition_frac"
    - "eval/search_r1_test/truncated_ratio"

# жӣІзәҝи§ЈиҜ»дёҺгҖҢжӯЈеёё/ејӮеёёгҖҚзӨәж„Ҹпјҡ
reading_guide:
  reward:
    train_raw:
      metric: "rollout/raw_reward"
      normal_shape: "зҹӯжңҹжңүеҷӘеЈ°пјҢдҪҶдёӯжңҹеқҮеҖјзј“ж…ўдёҠеҚҮжҲ–иҮіе°‘дёҚжҢҒз»ӯжҒ¶еҢ–гҖӮ"
      abnormal_shape:
        - "й•ҝжңҹиҙҙиҝ‘ 0 дё”ж— жіўеҠЁпјҡеёёи§ҒдәҺеҘ–еҠұиҝҮзЁҖз–ҸгҖҒж јејҸеҘ–еҠұжңӘе‘ҪдёӯгҖҒе·Ҙе…·и°ғз”ЁеӨұиҙҘзҺҮй«ҳгҖӮ"
        - "еүҚжңҹдёҠеҚҮеҗҺж–ӯеҙ–ејҸдёӢйҷҚпјҡеёёи§ҒдәҺеӯҰд№ зҺҮиҝҮеӨ§гҖҒKL/clip й…ҚзҪ®дёҚзЁігҖҒжҗңзҙўиҙЁйҮҸйӘӨйҷҚгҖӮ"
      notes:
        - "иҝҷжҳҜжңҖзӣҙи§Ӯзҡ„и®ӯз»ғеҘ–еҠұпјҢдјҳе…ҲзңӢе®ғгҖӮ"
    train_used:
      metric: "rollout/rewards"
      normal_shape: "GRPO дёӢеёёеӣ з»„еҶ…еҪ’дёҖеҢ–иҖҢеқҮеҖјжҺҘиҝ‘ 0пјҢжіўеҠЁжҜ” raw_reward жӣҙжҳҺжҳҫгҖӮ"
      abnormal_shape:
        - "еҮ д№ҺжҒ’дёә 0 дё”ж–№е·®жһҒе°ҸпјҡеҸҜиғҪ n_samples_per_prompt еӨӘе°ҸжҲ–еҘ–еҠұдҝЎеҸ·йҖҖеҢ–гҖӮ"
      notes:
        - "е®ғжҳҜеҸӮдёҺдјҳеҠҝи®Ўз®—зҡ„еҘ–еҠұпјҢдёҚзӯүдәҺеҺҹе§ӢеҘ–еҠұгҖӮ"
    eval:
      metric: "eval/search_r1_test"
      normal_shape: "йҡҸ eval step зј“ж…ўдёҠеҚҮжҲ–зЁіе®ҡпјӣе…Ғи®ёе°Ҹе№…еӣһж’ӨгҖӮ"
      abnormal_shape:
        - "жҢҒз»ӯдёӢйҷҚдё”ж— жі•жҒўеӨҚпјҡйҖҡеёёжҳҜзңҹжӯЈзҡ„жіӣеҢ–йҖҖеҢ–гҖӮ"
        - "еү§зғҲй”ҜйҪҝпјҡиҜ„дј°ж ·жң¬иҝҮе°‘жҲ–з”ҹжҲҗйҡҸжңәжҖ§иҝҮејәгҖӮ"

  loss:
    total:
      metric: "train/loss"
      normal_shape: "зј“ж…ўдёӢйҷҚжҲ–дҪҺе№…жіўеҠЁгҖӮ"
      abnormal_shape:
        - "зӘҒз„¶йЈҷеҚҮеҲ°жһҒеӨ§еҖј/NaNпјҡеёёи§ҒдәҺеӯҰд№ зҺҮиҝҮеӨ§гҖҒж•°еҖјдёҚзЁіе®ҡгҖҒжўҜеәҰзҲҶзӮёгҖӮ"
    pg:
      metric: "train/pg_loss"
      normal_shape: "дёҺжҖ» loss еҗҢжӯҘеҸҳеҢ–гҖӮ"
      abnormal_shape:
        - "й•ҝжңҹж— еҸҳеҢ–пјҡеҸҜиғҪзӯ–з•ҘеҮ д№ҺдёҚжӣҙж–°пјҲеӯҰд№ зҺҮеӨӘе°ҸгҖҒclip иҝҮејәгҖҒеҘ–еҠұж— еҢәеҲҶеәҰпјүгҖӮ"

  entropy:
    proxy:
      metric: "train/entropy_loss"
      normal_shape: "дёӯеҗҺжңҹзј“ж…ўдёӢйҷҚпјҢиЎЁзӨәзӯ–з•ҘйҖҗжӯҘж”¶ж•ӣгҖӮ"
      abnormal_shape:
        - "еҫҲеҝ«еЎҢеҲ°жҺҘиҝ‘ 0пјҡжҺўзҙўдёҚи¶іпјҢеҸҜиғҪиҝҮж—©ж”¶ж•ӣгҖӮ"
        - "жҢҒз»ӯеҫҲй«ҳдё”дёҚйҷҚпјҡзӯ–з•ҘеҸ‘ж•ЈжҲ–еӯҰд№ зҺҮеҒҸеӨ§гҖӮ"
      notes:
        - "иҜҘеҖјжҳҜжЎҶжһ¶и®°еҪ•зҡ„зҶөзӣёе…ійЎ№пјҲеёёдҪңдёәзӯ–з•ҘзҶөд»ЈзҗҶи§ӮеҜҹпјүгҖӮ"

  kl:
    ppo_kl:
      metric: "train/ppo_kl"
      normal_shape: "е°Ҹе№…жіўеҠЁпјҢж•ҙдҪ“дҝқжҢҒиҫғдҪҺгҖӮ"
      abnormal_shape:
        - "е‘ЁжңҹжҖ§е°–еі°еҫҲй«ҳпјҡзӯ–з•ҘжӯҘеӯҗиҝҮеӨ§пјҲlr й«ҳгҖҒclip й«ҳгҖҒеҘ–еҠұжіўеҠЁеӨ§пјүгҖӮ"
        - "й•ҝжңҹжҺҘиҝ‘ 0 дё” reward дёҚеҚҮпјҡзӯ–з•Ҙжӣҙж–°иҝҮејұгҖӮ"
    kl_loss:
      metric: "train/kl_loss"
      normal_shape: "д»…еҪ“ USE_KL_LOSS=1 ж—¶еҮәзҺ°пјӣеә”дёҺ kl_loss_coef еҚҸеҗҢзЁіе®ҡгҖӮ"
      abnormal_shape:
        - "жҳҺжҳҫжҢҒз»ӯжҠ¬еҚҮдё” reward дёӢйҷҚпјҡKL зәҰжқҹиҝҮејәжҲ–зӣ®ж ҮеҶІзӘҒгҖӮ"

  stability:
    grad_norm:
      metric: "train/grad_norm"
      normal_shape: "жңүжіўеҠЁдҪҶжҖ»дҪ“еҸҜжҺ§гҖӮ"
      abnormal_shape:
        - "йў‘з№Ғе°–еі°пјҡжўҜеәҰдёҚзЁіе®ҡпјҢе…ҲйҷҚ lr жҲ–еҠ ејә clipгҖӮ"
        - "й•ҝжңҹжҺҘиҝ‘ 0пјҡжӣҙж–°еӨӘејұпјҢеҸҜиғҪеӯҰдёҚеҠЁгҖӮ"
    truncation:
      metric: "rollout/truncated"
      normal_shape: "дёӯдҪҺж°ҙе№ігҖӮ"
      abnormal_shape:
        - "й•ҝжңҹеҒҸй«ҳпјҲеҰӮ >0.6пјүпјҡз”ҹжҲҗй•ҝеәҰдёҠйҷҗеҒҸе°ҸжҲ–еӣһзӯ”иҝҮй•ҝпјҢеҪұе“Қ reward дёҺеҸҜеӯҰжҖ§гҖӮ"

# дҪ еңЁ W&B зңӢдёҚеҲ° reward ж—¶пјҢжҢүиҝҷдёӘжё…еҚ•жҺ’жҹҘпјҡ
reward_not_found_checklist:
  - "еңЁ W&B жҗңзҙўжЎҶиҫ“е…Ҙпјҡrollout/raw_rewardпјҲи®ӯз»ғеҺҹе§ӢеҘ–еҠұпјү"
  - "еҶҚжҗңпјҡrollout/rewardsпјҲGRPO еҪ’дёҖеҢ–еҗҺеҘ–еҠұпјү"
  - "еҶҚжҗңпјҡeval/search_r1_testпјҲжөӢиҜ•йӣҶеҘ–еҠұпјү"
  - "зЎ®и®ӨеӣҫиЎЁ x иҪҙйҖүжӢ©дәҶеҜ№еә” stepпјҲrollout/step жҲ– eval/stepпјү"
  - "зЎ®и®Өи®ӯз»ғзЎ®е®һи§ҰеҸ‘дәҶ evalпјҲEVAL_INTERVAL з”ҹж•ҲпјҢдё”ж—Ҙеҝ—жңү eval_xx.ptпјү"

# еҪ“еүҚ Search-R1 зӨәдҫӢеҜ№гҖҢ429/Too Many RequestsгҖҚзҡ„е…ій”®й…ҚзҪ®йЎ№пјҡ
# иҝҷдәӣеҖјеңЁ full_train.yaml е’ҢеҗҜеҠЁи„ҡжң¬дёӯдјҡжіЁе…ҘиҝҗиЎҢзҺҜеўғгҖӮ
rate_limit_related_metrics:
  - "SEARCH_CONCURRENCY"
  - "GOOGLE_MIN_INTERVAL"
  - "GOOGLE_MAX_RETRIES"
  - "GOOGLE_BACKOFF_BASE"
  - "GOOGLE_BACKOFF_CAP"

# е»әи®®зҡ„ W&B йқўжқҝеёғеұҖпјҲдҫҝдәҺеҝ«йҖҹеҲӨж–ӯи®ӯз»ғжҳҜеҗҰжӯЈеёёпјүпјҡ
# 1) Reward з»„пјҡrollout/raw_reward + eval/search_r1_test
# 2) Policy з»„пјҡtrain/loss + train/pg_loss + train/entropy_loss + train/ppo_kl
# 3) Stability з»„пјҡtrain/grad_norm + rollout/truncated + rollout/response_lengths
dashboard_layout_hint:
  row1: ["rollout/raw_reward", "eval/search_r1_test"]
  row2: ["train/loss", "train/pg_loss", "train/entropy_loss", "train/ppo_kl"]
  row3: ["train/grad_norm", "rollout/truncated", "rollout/response_lengths"]

# ===================== PPO дё“ж Ҹ =====================
# иҜҙжҳҺпјҡ
# - еҪ“ ADVANTAGE_ESTIMATOR=ppo ж—¶пјҢжЎҶжһ¶дјҡиҮӘеҠЁеҗҜз”Ё criticгҖӮ
# - actor дёҺ critic зҡ„ train жҢҮж ҮдјҡеҲҶејҖи®°еҪ•пјҡ
#   actor:  train/loss, train/pg_loss, ...
#   critic: train/critic-value_loss, train/critic-value_clipfrac, ...
# - rollout дҫ§ reward/advantages/returns жҢҮж ҮдёҺ GRPO дёҖж ·еңЁ rollout/* дёӢгҖӮ
ppo_column:
  required_enable:
    - "ADVANTAGE_ESTIMATOR=ppo"
  key_metrics:
    reward:
      - "rollout/raw_reward"
      - "rollout/rewards"
      - "eval/search_r1_test"
    advantage_return:
      - "rollout/advantages"
      - "rollout/returns"
    actor_policy:
      - "train/loss"
      - "train/pg_loss"
      - "train/pg_clipfrac"
      - "train/ppo_kl"
      - "train/entropy_loss"
      - "train/grad_norm"
    critic_value:
      - "train/critic-value_loss"
      - "train/critic-value_clipfrac"
      - "train/critic-grad_norm"
      - "train/critic-lr-pg_0"
    optional:
      - "train/kl_loss"
      - "train/train_rollout_logprob_abs_diff"
      - "train/critic-lr-pg_1"

  how_to_read:
    reward:
      normal: "rollout/raw_reward дёҺ eval/search_r1_test дёӯжңҹзј“ж…ўдёҠеҚҮпјҢе…Ғи®ёзҹӯжңҹжіўеҠЁгҖӮ"
      abnormal:
        - "reward й•ҝжңҹиҙҙиҝ‘ 0пјҡеҘ–еҠұеҮҪж•°и§ҰеҸ‘дёҚи¶іжҲ–е·Ҙе…·и°ғз”Ё/ж јејҸеӨұиҙҘзҺҮй«ҳгҖӮ"
        - "reward еӨ§е№…йңҮиҚЎдё”еҗҢж—¶ ppo_kl е°–еі°пјҡзӯ–з•ҘжӯҘй•ҝиҝҮеӨ§пјҲlr/clip/KL йңҖж”¶ж•ӣпјүгҖӮ"
    advantage_return:
      normal: "rollout/advantages дёҺ rollout/returns еҲҶеёғжңүжіўеҠЁдҪҶдёҚиҝҮеәҰе°–еі°гҖӮ"
      abnormal:
        - "advantages й•ҝжңҹиҝ‘ 0пјҡеӯҰд№ дҝЎеҸ·ејұпјҢеҸҜиғҪйңҖиҰҒжҸҗй«ҳеҘ–еҠұеҢәеҲҶеәҰгҖӮ"
        - "advantages еҮәзҺ°жһҒз«Ҝе°–еі°пјҡйҖҡеёёжҳҜ reward/KL дёҚзЁіе®ҡжҲ–йҮҮж ·ејӮеёёгҖӮ"
    actor_policy:
      normal: "train/loss дёҺ train/pg_loss зј“ж…ўдёӢйҷҚпјӣtrain/ppo_kl з»ҙжҢҒдҪҺдҪҚжіўеҠЁгҖӮ"
      abnormal:
        - "train/ppo_kl жҢҒз»ӯиө°й«ҳе№¶дјҙйҡҸ reward дёӢж»‘пјҡзӯ–з•ҘжјӮз§»иҝҮеҝ«гҖӮ"
        - "train/entropy_loss еҝ«йҖҹеЎҢйҷ·еҲ°еҫҲдҪҺпјҡжҺўзҙўдёҚи¶ігҖӮ"
    critic_value:
      normal: "train/critic-value_loss дёӢйҷҚжҲ–и¶ӢзЁіпјҢvalue_clipfrac дёӯдҪҺдё”йҖҗжӯҘзЁіе®ҡгҖӮ"
      abnormal:

   690	    if [[ "${TEST_EXPORT_MODE}" == "final" ]]; then

   693	    elif [[ "${TEST_EXPORT_MODE}" != "all" ]]; then

   703	        continue
   704	      fi
   705	      bn="$(basename "${eval_pt}")"
   706	      rid="${bn#eval_}"

   708	      if [[ "${rid}" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]]; then

   714	      out_jsonl="${RUN_ROOT}/test_${step_id}.jsonl"
   715	      out_metrics="${RUN_ROOT}/test_${step_id}_metrics.json"
   716	      echo "Exporting eval trajectories from ${eval_pt} -> ${out_jsonl}"
   717	      python3 "${SEARCH_R1_DIR}/export_test_traj.py" \
   718	        --eval-rollout-pt "${eval_pt}" \

#!/usr/bin/env bash

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" &>/dev/null && pwd)"
DEFAULT_CONFIG_FILE="${SCRIPT_DIR}/configs/ppo_train.yaml"

# PPO й»ҳи®Өе…ҘеҸЈпјҡд»ҚеӨҚз”ЁеҺҹе§Ӣ Search-R1 rollout дёҺе·Ҙе…·и°ғз”ЁйҖ»иҫ‘гҖӮ
export CONFIG_FILE="${CONFIG_FILE:-${DEFAULT_CONFIG_FILE}}"
export ADVANTAGE_ESTIMATOR="${ADVANTAGE_ESTIMATOR:-ppo}"

# critic зӣёе…ій»ҳи®ӨеҖјпјҲеҸҜеңЁ YAML иҰҶзӣ–пјү
export NUM_CRITIC_ONLY_STEPS="${NUM_CRITIC_ONLY_STEPS:-0}"
export VALUE_CLIP="${VALUE_CLIP:-0.2}"

if [[ -z "${CRITIC_SAVE:-}" ]]; then
  ts="$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S)"
  export EXP_NAME="${EXP_NAME:-search-r1-ppo-google-${ts}}"
  # д»…еңЁжңӘжҳҫејҸи®ҫзҪ® CRITIC_SAVE ж—¶пјҢз»ҷдёҖдёӘзӢ¬з«Ӣзӣ®еҪ•пјҢйҒҝе…ҚдёҺ actor ckpt ж··ж·ҶгҖӮ
  export CRITIC_SAVE="${SCRIPT_DIR}/train_log/${EXP_NAME}/ckpt_critic"
fi

exec "${SCRIPT_DIR}/run_qwen2.5_3B_google_grpo_jkh.sh" "$@"

import argparse

from huggingface_hub import hf_hub_download

parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Download files from a Hugging Face dataset repository.")
parser.add_argument("--repo_id", type=str, default="PeterJinGo/wiki-18-e5-index", help="Hugging Face repository ID")
parser.add_argument("--save_path", type=str, required=True, help="Local directory to save files")

args = parser.parse_args()

repo_id = "PeterJinGo/wiki-18-e5-index"
for file in ["part_aa", "part_ab"]:
    hf_hub_download(
        repo_id=repo_id,
        filename=file,  # e.g., "e5_Flat.index"
        repo_type="dataset",
        local_dir=args.save_path,
    )

repo_id = "PeterJinGo/wiki-18-corpus"
hf_hub_download(
    repo_id=repo_id,
    filename="wiki-18.jsonl.gz",
    repo_type="dataset",
    local_dir=args.save_path,
)

import argparse
import json
import warnings

import datasets
import faiss
import numpy as np
import torch
import uvicorn
from fastapi import FastAPI
from pydantic import BaseModel
from tqdm import tqdm
from transformers import AutoModel, AutoTokenizer


def load_corpus(corpus_path: str):
    corpus = datasets.load_dataset("json", data_files=corpus_path, split="train", num_proc=4)
    return corpus


def load_docs(corpus, doc_idxs):
    results = [corpus[int(idx)] for idx in doc_idxs]
    return results


def load_model(model_path: str, use_fp16: bool = False):
    model = AutoModel.from_pretrained(model_path, trust_remote_code=True)
    model.eval()
    model.cuda()
    if use_fp16:
        model = model.half()
    tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(model_path, use_fast=True, trust_remote_code=True)
    return model, tokenizer


def pooling(pooler_output, last_hidden_state, attention_mask=None, pooling_method="mean"):
    if pooling_method == "mean":
        last_hidden = last_hidden_state.masked_fill(~attention_mask[..., None].bool(), 0.0)
        return last_hidden.sum(dim=1) / attention_mask.sum(dim=1)[..., None]
    elif pooling_method == "cls":
        return last_hidden_state[:, 0]
    elif pooling_method == "pooler":
        return pooler_output
    else:
        raise NotImplementedError("Pooling method not implemented!")


class Encoder:
    def __init__(self, model_name, model_path, pooling_method, max_length, use_fp16):
        self.model_name = model_name
        self.model_path = model_path
        self.pooling_method = pooling_method
        self.max_length = max_length
        self.use_fp16 = use_fp16

        self.model, self.tokenizer = load_model(model_path=model_path, use_fp16=use_fp16)
        self.model.eval()

    @torch.no_grad()
    def encode(self, query_list: list[str], is_query=True) -> np.ndarray:
        # processing query for different encoders
        if isinstance(query_list, str):
            query_list = [query_list]

        if "e5" in self.model_name.lower():
            if is_query:
                query_list = [f"query: {query}" for query in query_list]
            else:
                query_list = [f"passage: {query}" for query in query_list]

        if "bge" in self.model_name.lower():
            if is_query:
                query_list = [
                    f"Represent this sentence for searching relevant passages: {query}" for query in query_list
                ]

        inputs = self.tokenizer(
            query_list, max_length=self.max_length, padding=True, truncation=True, return_tensors="pt"
        )
        inputs = {k: v.cuda() for k, v in inputs.items()}

        if "T5" in type(self.model).__name__:
            # T5-based retrieval model
            decoder_input_ids = torch.zeros((inputs["input_ids"].shape[0], 1), dtype=torch.long).to(
                inputs["input_ids"].device
            )
            output = self.model(**inputs, decoder_input_ids=decoder_input_ids, return_dict=True)
            query_emb = output.last_hidden_state[:, 0, :]
        else:
            output = self.model(**inputs, return_dict=True)
            query_emb = pooling(
                output.pooler_output, output.last_hidden_state, inputs["attention_mask"], self.pooling_method
            )
            if "dpr" not in self.model_name.lower():
                query_emb = torch.nn.functional.normalize(query_emb, dim=-1)

        query_emb = query_emb.detach().cpu().numpy()
        query_emb = query_emb.astype(np.float32, order="C")

        del inputs, output
        torch.cuda.empty_cache()

        return query_emb


class BaseRetriever:
    def __init__(self, config):
        self.config = config
        self.retrieval_method = config.retrieval_method
        self.topk = config.retrieval_topk

        self.index_path = config.index_path
        self.corpus_path = config.corpus_path

    def _search(self, query: str, num: int, return_score: bool):
        raise NotImplementedError

    def _batch_search(self, query_list: list[str], num: int, return_score: bool):
        raise NotImplementedError

    def search(self, query: str, num: int = None, return_score: bool = False):
        return self._search(query, num, return_score)

    def batch_search(self, query_list: list[str], num: int = None, return_score: bool = False):
        return self._batch_search(query_list, num, return_score)


class BM25Retriever(BaseRetriever):
    def __init__(self, config):
        super().__init__(config)
        from pyserini.search.lucene import LuceneSearcher

        self.searcher = LuceneSearcher(self.index_path)
        self.contain_doc = self._check_contain_doc()
        if not self.contain_doc:
            self.corpus = load_corpus(self.corpus_path)
        self.max_process_num = 8

    def _check_contain_doc(self):
        return self.searcher.doc(0).raw() is not None

    def _search(self, query: str, num: int = None, return_score: bool = False):
        if num is None:
            num = self.topk
        hits = self.searcher.search(query, num)
        if len(hits) < 1:
            if return_score:
                return [], []
            else:
                return []
        scores = [hit.score for hit in hits]
        if len(hits) < num:
            warnings.warn("Not enough documents retrieved!", stacklevel=2)
        else:
            hits = hits[:num]

        if self.contain_doc:
            all_contents = [json.loads(self.searcher.doc(hit.docid).raw())["contents"] for hit in hits]
            results = [
                {
                    "title": content.split("\n")[0].strip('"'),
                    "text": "\n".join(content.split("\n")[1:]),
                    "contents": content,
                }
                for content in all_contents
            ]
        else:
            results = load_docs(self.corpus, [hit.docid for hit in hits])

        if return_score:
            return results, scores
        else:
            return results

    def _batch_search(self, query_list: list[str], num: int = None, return_score: bool = False):
        results = []
        scores = []
        for query in query_list:
            item_result, item_score = self._search(query, num, True)
            results.append(item_result)
            scores.append(item_score)
        if return_score:
            return results, scores
        else:
            return results


class DenseRetriever(BaseRetriever):
    def __init__(self, config):
        super().__init__(config)
        self.index = faiss.read_index(self.index_path)
        if config.faiss_gpu:
            co = faiss.GpuMultipleClonerOptions()
            co.useFloat16 = True
            co.shard = True
            self.index = faiss.index_cpu_to_all_gpus(self.index, co=co)

        self.corpus = load_corpus(self.corpus_path)
        self.encoder = Encoder(
            model_name=self.retrieval_method,
            model_path=config.retrieval_model_path,
            pooling_method=config.retrieval_pooling_method,
            max_length=config.retrieval_query_max_length,
            use_fp16=config.retrieval_use_fp16,
        )
        self.topk = config.retrieval_topk
        self.batch_size = config.retrieval_batch_size

    def _search(self, query: str, num: int = None, return_score: bool = False):
        if num is None:
            num = self.topk
        query_emb = self.encoder.encode(query)
        scores, idxs = self.index.search(query_emb, k=num)
        idxs = idxs[0]
        scores = scores[0]
        results = load_docs(self.corpus, idxs)
        if return_score:
            return results, scores.tolist()
        else:
            return results

    def _batch_search(self, query_list: list[str], num: int = None, return_score: bool = False):
        if isinstance(query_list, str):
            query_list = [query_list]
        if num is None:
            num = self.topk

        results = []
        scores = []
        for start_idx in tqdm(range(0, len(query_list), self.batch_size), desc="Retrieval process: "):
            query_batch = query_list[start_idx : start_idx + self.batch_size]
            batch_emb = self.encoder.encode(query_batch)
            batch_scores, batch_idxs = self.index.search(batch_emb, k=num)
            batch_scores = batch_scores.tolist()
            batch_idxs = batch_idxs.tolist()

            # load_docs is not vectorized, but is a python list approach
            flat_idxs = sum(batch_idxs, [])
            batch_results = load_docs(self.corpus, flat_idxs)
            # chunk them back
            batch_results = [batch_results[i * num : (i + 1) * num] for i in range(len(batch_idxs))]

            results.extend(batch_results)
            scores.extend(batch_scores)

            del batch_emb, batch_scores, batch_idxs, query_batch, flat_idxs, batch_results
            torch.cuda.empty_cache()

        if return_score:
            return results, scores
        else:
            return results


def get_retriever(config):
    if config.retrieval_method == "bm25":
        return BM25Retriever(config)
    else:
        return DenseRetriever(config)

class Config:
    """
    Minimal config class (simulating your argparse)
    Replace this with your real arguments or load them dynamically.
    """

    def __init__(
        self,
        retrieval_method: str = "bm25",
        retrieval_topk: int = 10,
        index_path: str = "./index/bm25",
        corpus_path: str = "./data/corpus.jsonl",
        dataset_path: str = "./data",
        data_split: str = "train",
        faiss_gpu: bool = True,
        retrieval_model_path: str = "./model",
        retrieval_pooling_method: str = "mean",
        retrieval_query_max_length: int = 256,
        retrieval_use_fp16: bool = False,
        retrieval_batch_size: int = 128,
    ):
        self.retrieval_method = retrieval_method
        self.retrieval_topk = retrieval_topk
        self.index_path = index_path
        self.corpus_path = corpus_path
        self.dataset_path = dataset_path
        self.data_split = data_split
        self.faiss_gpu = faiss_gpu
        self.retrieval_model_path = retrieval_model_path
        self.retrieval_pooling_method = retrieval_pooling_method
        self.retrieval_query_max_length = retrieval_query_max_length
        self.retrieval_use_fp16 = retrieval_use_fp16
        self.retrieval_batch_size = retrieval_batch_size


class QueryRequest(BaseModel):
    queries: list[str]
    topk: int | None = None
    return_scores: bool = False


app = FastAPI()


@app.post("/retrieve")
def retrieve_endpoint(request: QueryRequest):
    """
    Endpoint that accepts queries and performs retrieval.

    Input format:
    {
      "queries": ["What is Python?", "Tell me about neural networks."],
      "topk": 3,
      "return_scores": true
    }

    Output format (when return_scores=TrueпјҢsimilarity scores are returned):
    {
        "result": [
            [   # Results for each query
                {
                    {"document": doc, "score": score}
                },
                # ... more documents
            ],
            # ... results for other queries
        ]
    }
    """
    if not request.topk:
        request.topk = config.retrieval_topk  # fallback to default

    # Perform batch retrieval
    tmp = retriever.batch_search(query_list=request.queries, num=request.topk, return_score=request.return_scores)

    scores = []
    try:
        results, scores = tmp
    except ValueError:
        results = tmp

    # Format response
    resp = []
    for i, single_result in enumerate(results):
        if scores:
            # If scores are returned, combine them with results
            combined = []
            for doc, score in zip(single_result, scores[i], strict=True):
                combined.append({"document": doc, "score": score})
            resp.append(combined)
        else:
            resp.append(single_result)
    return {"result": resp}

е…ҲжҠҠ/mnt/workspace/jkh/slimeзӣ®еҪ•дҪңдёәдёҖдёӘgithubйЎ№зӣ®пјҢжҠҠиҝҷдёӘйЎ№зӣ®зҡ„е®үиЈ…дҫқиө–пјҢеҰӮдҪ•жҲҗеҠҹзҡ„еңЁе…¶д»–и®ҫеӨҮдёҠиҝҗиЎҢиҝҷдёӘж–Үд»¶йҮҢзҡ„и®ӯз»ғи„ҡжң¬еҶҷдёҖд»ҪиҜҰз»ҶиҜҙжҳҺпјҢжҲҗдёҖд»Ҫж–°зҡ„mdж–ҮжЎЈеңЁ/mnt/workspace/jkh/slimeдёӢпјҢе‘ҪеҗҚдёәinstall.mdпјҢдёҠдј жүҖжңүж–Үд»¶еҲ°https://github.co/inmnin/coupile-r1.gitзҡ„дё»еҲҶж”ҜпјҢиҝҷжҳҜжҲ‘зҡ„дёҖдёӘз§Ғжңүеә“пјҢеҶҚж¬Ўд№ӢеүҚйңҖиҰҒgithubи®ӨиҜҒиҜ·е…ҲдёҺжҲ‘зЎ®и®ӨпјҢдҪҶжҳҜйҒҝе…ҚдёҠдј еӨ§jsonl
ж•°жҚ®ж–Үд»¶пјҢжҜ”еҰӮжүҖжңүеӨ§jsonlж–Үд»¶д»ҺйҮҢйқўжҠҪ2eжқЎе…ёеһӢзҡ„jsonж•°жҚ®иҝӣиЎҢдёҠдј гҖӮз„¶еҗҺеҶҚиҝӣиЎҢеҗҺз»ӯе·ҘдҪңпјҡжҲ‘д»ҘеүҚз”ЁиҝҮcodexеҜ№иҝҷдёӘеә“еҒҡдәҶеҫҲеӨҡе·ҘдҪң/mnt/workspace/jkh/slimeпјҢдҪ еҸҜд»ҘйҖҡиҝҮжңҖиҝ‘дҝ®ж”№иҝҮзҡ„readme.mdжқҘеӨ§жҰӮзңӢдёҖдёӢдҪ 
еҒҡиҝҮд»Җд№ҲпјҢз„¶еҗҺ/mnt/workspace/jkh/slime/examples/search-r1жҲ‘д»¬еҜ№иҝҷдёӘеә“иҝӣиЎҢдәҶеӨ§йҮҸзҡ„дҝ®ж”№пјҢжңҖеҗҺзҡ„е·ҘдҪңжҳҜдёәдәҶиҝӣиЎҢgrpoи®ӯз»ғиҖҢиҝӣиЎҢеҶ·еҗҜеҠЁж•°жҚ®зҡ„и’ёйҰҸпјҢд№ӢеүҚжңүдёӘtmuxзҡ„иҝӣзЁӢзҺ°еңЁеҸҜиғҪжӯ»дәҶпјҢе®ғдёҖзӣҙеңЁжү§иЎҢжңҖж–°зҡ„и’ё
йҰҸиҝӣзЁӢпјҢиҜ·дҪ иө·дёҖдёӘеҗҚеӯ—дёәjkhзҡ„tmuxзӘ—еҸЈпјҢз„¶еҗҺйҮҚж–°жҒўеӨҚжү§иЎҢиҝҷдёӘжңҖж–°зҡ„иҝӣзЁӢпјҲеҜ№жІЎжңүidиҰҶзӣ–зҡ„ж•°жҚ®иҝӣиЎҢз»§з»ӯи’ёйҰҸпјүпјҢйЎ№зӣ®йҮҢдҪ еҸҜд»Ҙж №жҚ®дҝ®ж”№ж—¶й—ҙзңӢе“ӘдәӣжҳҜйҮҚиҰҒзҡ„ж–Үд»¶пјҢз„¶еҗҺеә“йҮҢеҸҜиғҪдјҡз•ҷдёӢдёҖдәӣ1ogпјҢиҜ·дҪ е°Ҫеҝ«йҖӮеә”иҝҷдёӘ
д»»еҠЎзҺҜеўғпјҢдҪ еңЁжү§иЎҢи’ёйҰҸд»»еҠЎеүҚпјҢиҰҒеҜ№и’ёйҰҸзҡ„pipelineпјҢе№¶еҸ‘ж•°пјҢзӯӣйҖүиҰҒжұӮзӯүеҗ„дёӘиҜҰз»Ҷжғ…еҶөи·ҹжҲ‘иҝӣиЎҢзЎ®и®ӨпјҢзЎ®и®Өе®ҢдәҶдҪ е°ұеҸҜд»Ҙжү§иЎҢдәҶпјҢз»§з»ӯжү§иЎҢ

# AGENTS.md instructions for /mnt/workspace/jkh

<INSTRUCTIONS>
## Skills
A skill is a set of local instructions to follow that is stored in a `SKILL.md` file. Below is the list of skills that can be used. Each entry includes a name, description, and file path so you can open the source for full instructions when using a specific skill.
### Available skills
- skill-creator: Guide for creating effective skills. This skill should be used when users want to create a new skill (or update an existing skill) that extends Codex's capabilities with specialized knowledge, workflows, or tool integrations. (file: /root/.codex/skills/.system/skill-creator/SKILL.md)
- skill-installer: Install Codex skills into $CODEX_HOME/skills from a curated list or a GitHub repo path. Use when a user asks to list installable skills, install a curated skill, or install a skill from another repo (including private repos). (file: /root/.codex/skills/.system/skill-installer/SKILL.md)
### How to use skills
- Discovery: The list above is the skills available in this session (name + description + file path). Skill bodies live on disk at the listed paths.
- Trigger rules: If the user names a skill (with `$SkillName` or plain text) OR the task clearly matches a skill's description shown above, you must use that skill for that turn. Multiple mentions mean use them all. Do not carry skills across turns unless re-mentioned.
- Missing/blocked: If a named skill isn't in the list or the path can't be read, say so briefly and continue with the best fallback.
- How to use a skill (progressive disclosure):
  1) After deciding to use a skill, open its `SKILL.md`. Read only enough to follow the workflow.
  2) When `SKILL.md` references relative paths (e.g., `scripts/foo.py`), resolve them relative to the skill directory listed above first, and only consider other paths if needed.
  3) If `SKILL.md` points to extra folders such as `references/`, load only the specific files needed for the request; don't bulk-load everything.
  4) If `scripts/` exist, prefer running or patching them instead of retyping large code blocks.
  5) If `assets/` or templates exist, reuse them instead of recreating from scratch.
- Coordination and sequencing:
  - If multiple skills apply, choose the minimal set that covers the request and state the order you'll use them.
  - Announce which skill(s) you're using and why (one short line). If you skip an obvious skill, say why.
- Context hygiene:
  - Keep context small: summarize long sections instead of pasting them; only load extra files when needed.
  - Avoid deep reference-chasing: prefer opening only files directly linked from `SKILL.md` unless you're blocked.
  - When variants exist (frameworks, providers, domains), pick only the relevant reference file(s) and note that choice.
- Safety and fallback: If a skill can't be applied cleanly (missing files, unclear instructions), state the issue, pick the next-best approach, and continue.
</INSTRUCTIONS>

  - If multiple skills apply, choose the minimal set that covers the request and state the order you'll use them.
  - Announce which skill(s) you're using and why (one short line). If you skip an obvious skill, say why.
- Context hygiene:
  - Keep context small: summarize long sections instead of pasting them; only load extra files when needed.
  - Avoid deep reference-chasing: prefer opening only files directly linked from `SKILL.md` unless you're blocked.
  - When variants exist (frameworks, providers, domains), pick only the relevant reference file(s) and note that choice.
- Safety and fallback: If a skill can't be applied cleanly (missing files, unclear instructions), state the issue, pick the next-best approach, and continue.

     1	# Search-R1 lite
     2	
     3	This is a minimal reproduction of [Search-R1](https://github.com/PeterGriffinJin/Search-R1) and an example of using multi-turn conversation and tool-calling in slime.
     4	
     5	## Environment Setup
     6	
     7	Use the `slimerl/slime:latest` image and initialize the environment required for Search-R1:
     8	
     9	```bash
    10	cd /root/
    11	git clone https://github.com/THUDM/slime.git
    12	pip install -e . --no-deps
    13	# for Search R1
    14	pip install chardet
    15	```
    16	
    17	Download and prepare the training data:
    18	
    19	```bash
    20	cd /root/
    21	git clone https://github.com/PeterGriffinJin/Search-R1.git
    22	cd Search-R1/
    23	pip install -e . --no-deps
    24	pip install tensordict
    25	
    26	# Set your working directory
    27	WORK_DIR=/root/Search-R1
    28	LOCAL_DIR=$WORK_DIR/data/nq_hotpotqa_train
    29	
    30	# Process multiple dataset search format train file
    31	DATA=nq,hotpotqa
    32	python $WORK_DIR/scripts/data_process/qa_search_train_merge.py \
    33	    --local_dir $LOCAL_DIR \
    34	    --data_sources $DATA
    35	
    36	# (Optional) Process multiple dataset search format test file
    37	# Note: the final file is not shuffled
    38	DATA=nq,triviaqa,popqa,hotpotqa,2wikimultihopqa,musique,bamboogle
    39	python $WORK_DIR/scripts/data_process/qa_search_test_merge.py \
    40	    --local_dir $LOCAL_DIR \
    41	    --data_sources $DATA
    42	```
    43	
    44	**Note:** If you plan to use local search backend, see the [Appendix](#appendix-setting-up-local-retriever) for instructions on setting up the local retrieval server.
    45	
    46	Initialize the Qwen2.5-3B model:
    47	
    48	```bash
    49	# hf checkpoint
    50	hf download Qwen/Qwen2.5-3B --local-dir /root/Qwen2.5-3B
    51	
    52	# mcore checkpoint
    53	cd /root/slime
    54	source scripts/models/qwen2.5-3B.sh
    55	PYTHONPATH=/root/Megatron-LM python tools/convert_hf_to_torch_dist.py \
    56	    ${MODEL_ARGS[@]} \
    57	    --hf-checkpoint /root/Qwen2.5-3B \
    58	    --save /root/Qwen2.5-3B_torch_dist
    59	```
    60	
    61	## Configuration
    62	
    63	### Search Backend Configuration
    64	
    65	The `generate_with_search.py` file supports both **local search** and **Google search** backends. Configure via the `SEARCH_R1_CONFIGS` dictionary:
    66	
    67	```python
    68	SEARCH_R1_CONFIGS = {
    69	    # ============== General Configuration ==============
    70	    "max_turns": 2,
    71	    "topk": 3,
    72	    "search_concurrency": 256,
    73	
    74	    # ============== Search Backend Selection ==============
    75	    "search_backend": "local",  # Options: "local" or "google"
    76	
    77	    # ============== Local Search Configuration ==============
    78	    # (Only used when search_backend="local")
    79	    "local": {
    80	        "search_url": "http://127.0.0.1:8000/retrieve",  # URL of your local retrieval server
    81	        "proxy": None,
    82	    },
    83	
    84	    # ============== Google Search Configuration ==============
    85	    # (Only used when search_backend="google")
    86	    "google": {
    87	        "api_key": "your_api_key_here",  # Replace with your actual serper.dev API key
    88	        "snippet_only": True,
    89	        "proxy": None,
    90	    },
    91	
    92	    # ============== Log Probability Collection ==============
    93	    "return_logprob": True,  # Set to True to collect log probabilities (required for TIS)
    94	
    95	    # ============== Reward Model Configuration ==============
    96	    "format_score": 0.2,
    97	}
    98	```
    99	
   100	#### Using Local Search
   101	
   102	1. Set `"search_backend": "local"`
   103	2. Configure `"local"` section with your local retrieval server URL
   104	3. Start your local search server before running the training script
   105	
   106	#### Using Google Search
   107	
   108	1. Set `"search_backend": "google"`
   109	2. Configure `"google"` section with your serper.dev API key
   110	3. Get your API key from [serper.dev](https://serper.dev)
   111	
   112	### Enabling TIS (Trajectory Importance Sampling)
   113	
   114	TIS requires log probability collection. To enable TIS:
   115	
   116	**1. In `generate_with_search.py`:**
   117	```python
   118	SEARCH_R1_CONFIGS = {
   119	    # ... other configs
   120	    "return_logprob": True,  # Must be True for TIS
   121	}
   122	```
   123	
   124	**2. In `run_qwen2.5_3B.sh`:**
   125	
   126	Uncomment the TIS-related arguments in `GRPO_ARGS`:
   127	```bash
   128	GRPO_ARGS=(
   129	   --advantage-estimator grpo
   130	   --use-kl-loss
   131	   --kl-loss-coef 0.001
   132	   --kl-loss-type low_var_kl
   133	   --entropy-coef 0.00
   134	   --eps-clip 0.2
   135	   --eps-clip-high 0.28
   136	
   137	   # Uncomment to enable TIS
   138	   --use-tis
   139	)
   140	```
   141	
   142	And uncomment the TIS configuration paths in `CUSTOM_ARGS`:
   143	```bash
   144	CUSTOM_ARGS=(
   145	   --custom-generate-function-path generate_with_search.generate
   146	   --custom-rm-path generate_with_search.reward_func
   147	
   148	   # Uncomment to enable TIS
   149	   --custom-config-path examples/train_infer_mismatch_helper/mis.yaml
   150	   --custom-tis-function-path examples.train_infer_mismatch_helper.mis.compute_mis_weights_with_cp
   151	)
   152	```
   153	
   154	**Important Notes:**
   155	- TIS requires `return_logprob=True` in `SEARCH_R1_CONFIGS`
   156	- When collecting log probabilities, response postprocessing is automatically disabled to maintain token/logp alignment
   157	- TIS adds computational overhead but can improve training efficiency
   158	
   159	## Running the Script
   160	
   161	```bash
   162	cd slime/
   163	bash examples/search-r1/run_qwen2.5_3B.sh
   164	```
   165	
   166	## Code Structure
   167	
   168	To implement multi-turn conversation + tool-calling in slime, you only need to implement a custom data generation function and a reward model for the task. These correspond to the following 2 configuration items in the startup script:
   169	
   170	```bash
   171	CUSTOM_ARGS=(
   172	   --custom-generate-function-path generate_with_search.generate
   173	   --custom-rm-path generate_with_search.reward_func
   174	)
   175	```
   176	
   177	These are the `generate` and `reward_func` functions in `generate_with_search.py`.
   178	
   179	## Appendix: Setting up Local Retriever
   180	
   181	This section provides detailed instructions for setting up the local dense retriever for use with the local search backend.
   182	
   183	### Prerequisites
   184	
   185	The local retriever requires a separate conda environment to avoid conflicts with the training environment. It uses GPU for efficient retrieval.
   186	
   187	### Step 1: Install Conda
   188	
   189	If you don't have conda installed, run the following commands:
   190	
   191	```bash
   192	# Download and install conda
   193	wget https://repo.anaconda.com/miniconda/Miniconda3-latest-Linux-x86_64.sh -O ~/miniconda.sh
   194	bash ~/miniconda.sh -b -p $HOME/miniconda3
   195	source ~/miniconda3/etc/profile.d/conda.sh
   196	conda init
   197	source ~/.bashrc
   198	
   199	# Accept conda terms of service
   200	conda tos accept --override-channels --channel https://repo.anaconda.com/pkgs/main
   201	conda tos accept --override-channels --channel https://repo.anaconda.com/pkgs/r
   202	```
   203	
   204	### Step 2: Create Retriever Environment
   205	
   206	Create and activate a conda environment with Python 3.10:
   207	
   208	```bash
   209	# Create environment
   210	conda create -n retriever python=3.10 -y
   211	conda activate retriever
   212	
   213	# Install PyTorch with CUDA support
   214	conda install pytorch==2.4.0 torchvision==0.19.0 torchaudio==2.4.0 pytorch-cuda=12.1 -c pytorch -c nvidia -y
   215	
   216	# Install required packages
   217	pip install transformers datasets pyserini huggingface_hub
   218	conda install faiss-gpu=1.8.0 -c pytorch -c nvidia -y
   219	pip install uvicorn fastapi
   220	```
   221	
   222	### Step 3: Download Index and Corpus
   223	
   224	**Note:** The local retrieval files are large. You'll need approximately **60-70 GB** for download and **132 GB** after extraction. Make sure you have sufficient disk space.
   225	
   226	```bash
   227	# Set your save path
   228	save_path=/root/Index
   229	
   230	# Download the index and corpus files
   231	python /root/slime/examples/search-r1/local_dense_retriever/download.py --save_path $save_path
   232	
   233	# Combine split index files
   234	cat $save_path/part_* > $save_path/e5_Flat.index
   235	
   236	# Decompress the corpus
   237	gzip -d $save_path/wiki-18.jsonl.gz
   238	```
   239	
   240	### Step 4: Start Local Retrieval Server
   241	
   242	```bash
   243	# If you encounter "conda not found" error, run:
   244	# source ~/miniconda3/etc/profile.d/conda.sh
   245	# conda init
   246	# source ~/.bashrc
   247	
   248	# Activate retriever environment
   249	conda activate retriever
   250	
   251	# Set paths
   252	save_path=/root/Index
   253	index_file=$save_path/e5_Flat.index
   254	corpus_file=$save_path/wiki-18.jsonl
   255	retriever_name=e5
   256	retriever_path=intfloat/e5-base-v2
   257	
   258	# Start the retrieval server
   259	python /root/slime/examples/search-r1/local_dense_retriever/retrieval_server.py \
   260	    --index_path $index_file \

    14	# Configuration for Search-R1
    15	def _env_bool(key: str, default: bool) -> bool:
    16	    value = os.environ.get(key)
    17	    if value is None:
    18	        return default
    19	    return value.strip().lower() in ("1", "true", "yes", "on")
    20	
    21	
    22	def _env_int(key: str, default: int) -> int:
    23	    value = os.environ.get(key)
    24	    return int(value) if value is not None and value != "" else default
    25	
    26	
    27	def _env_float(key: str, default: float) -> float:
    28	    value = os.environ.get(key)
    29	    return float(value) if value is not None and value != "" else default
    30	
    31	
    32	SEARCH_R1_CONFIGS = {
    33	    # ============== General Configuration ==============
    34	    "max_turns": _env_int("SEARCH_R1_MAX_TURNS", 2),
    35	    "topk": _env_int("SEARCH_R1_TOPK", 3),
    36	    "search_concurrency": _env_int("SEARCH_R1_SEARCH_CONCURRENCY", 32),
    37	    # ============== Search Backend Selection ==============
    38	    "search_backend": os.environ.get("SEARCH_R1_SEARCH_BACKEND", "google"),  # Options: "local" or "google"
    39	    # ============== Local Search Configuration ==============
    40	    # (Only used when search_backend="local")
    41	    "local": {
    42	        "search_url": os.environ.get("SEARCH_R1_LOCAL_SEARCH_URL", "http://127.0.0.1:8000/retrieve"),
    43	        "proxy": os.environ.get("SEARCH_R1_LOCAL_PROXY") or None,
    44	    },
    45	    # ============== Google Search Configuration ==============
    46	    # (Only used when search_backend="google")
    47	    "google": {
    48	        "api_key": os.environ.get("SEARCH_R1_SERPER_API_KEY", os.environ.get("SERPER_API_KEY", "")),
    49	        "snippet_only": _env_bool("SEARCH_R1_GOOGLE_SNIPPET_ONLY", True),
    50	        "proxy": os.environ.get("SEARCH_R1_GOOGLE_PROXY") or None,
    51	    },
    52	    # ============== Log Probability Collection ==============
    53	    "return_logprob": _env_bool("SEARCH_R1_RETURN_LOGPROB", True),

    59	SEMAPHORE = asyncio.Semaphore(SEARCH_R1_CONFIGS["search_concurrency"])

    61	
    62	def _passages2string(retrieval_result):
    63	    """
    64	    Convert retrieval results to a formatted string.
    65	    This function works with both google_search and local_search results.
    66	    """
    67	    format_reference = ""
    68	    for idx, doc_item in enumerate(retrieval_result):
    69	        content = doc_item["document"]["contents"]
    70	        title = content.split("\n")[0]
    71	        text = "\n".join(content.split("\n")[1:])
    72	        format_reference += f"Doc {idx+1}(Title: {title}) {text}\n"
    73	
    74	    return format_reference
    75

    77	async def search(query: str) -> str:
    78	    """
    79	    Perform search using either local search engine or Google search.
    80	    The search backend is determined by SEARCH_R1_CONFIGS["search_backend"].
    81	    """
    82	    backend = SEARCH_R1_CONFIGS["search_backend"].strip().lower()
    83	
    84	    if backend == "local":
    85	        from local_search_server import local_search
    86	
    87	        local_config = SEARCH_R1_CONFIGS["local"]
    88	        result = await local_search(
    89	            local_config["search_url"],
    90	            query,
    91	            SEARCH_R1_CONFIGS["topk"],
    92	            proxy=local_config["proxy"],
    93	        )
    94	    elif backend == "google":
    95	        from google_search_server import google_search
    96	
    97	        google_config = SEARCH_R1_CONFIGS["google"]
    98	        if not google_config["api_key"]:
    99	            raise ValueError("SEARCH_R1_SERPER_API_KEY is empty but search backend is set to google.")
   100	        result = await google_search(
   101	            google_config["api_key"],
   102	            query,
   103	            SEARCH_R1_CONFIGS["topk"],
   104	            snippet_only=google_config["snippet_only"],
   105	            proxy=google_config["proxy"],
   106	        )
   107	    else:
   108	        raise ValueError(f"Unknown search backend: {backend}. " f"Must be either 'local' or 'google'.")
   109	
   110	    return _passages2string(result)
   111	
   112	
   113	# IMPORTANT: When we need to collect log probabilities (logp), we CANNOT do any postprocessing
   114	# on the strings returned from the inference engine (sglang). This is because:
   115	# 1. We don't know how to truncate the corresponding tokens/logp arrays to match the modified string
   116	# 2. Re-tokenizing the postprocessed string may produce different tokens than what the engine generated,
   117	#    leading to misalignment between tokens and their log probabilities
   118	# Therefore, postprocess_responses is only used when return_logprob=False.
   119	def postprocess_responses(resp: str) -> str:
   120	    """
   121	    Post-process response to ensure tag completeness.
   122	    Only used when SEARCH_R1_CONFIGS["return_logprob"] is False.
   123	    """
   124	    return (
   125	        resp.split("</search>")[0] + "</search>"
   126	        if "</search>" in resp
   127	        else resp.split("</answer>")[0] + "</answer>" if "</answer>" in resp else resp
   128	    )
   129	
   130	
   131	def postprocess_predictions(prediction: str):
   132	    pattern = r"<(search|answer)>(.*?)</\1>"
   133	    match = re.search(pattern, prediction, re.DOTALL)
   134	    if match:
   135	        content = match.group(2).strip()  # Return only the content inside the tags
   136	        action = match.group(1)
   137	    else:
   138	        content = ""
   139	        action = None
   140	
   141	    return action, content
   142	
   143	
   144	async def execute_predictions(prediction: str) -> str:
   145	    action, content = postprocess_predictions(prediction)
   146	
   147	    if action == "search":
   148	        search_query = content
   149	        try:
   150	            async with SEMAPHORE:
   151	                search_results = await search(search_query)
   152	        except Exception as exc:
   153	            # Keep rollout alive on transient tool failures (e.g. provider throttling).
   154	            print(f"[search-r1] search failed ({type(exc).__name__}): {exc}", flush=True)
   155	            search_results = "No search results due to temporary search error."
   156	        next_obs = f"\n\n<information>{search_results.strip()}</information>\n\n"
   157	        done = False
   158	    elif action == "answer":
   159	        next_obs = ""
   160	        done = True
   161	    else:
   162	        next_obs = "\nMy previous action is invalid. \
   163	If I want to search, I should put the query between <search> and </search>. \
   164	If I want to give the final answer, I should put the answer between <answer> and </answer>. Let me try again.\n"
   165	        done = False
   166	
   167	    return next_obs, done

   170	async def generate(args, sample: Sample, sampling_params) -> Sample:

    44	async def fetch(session: aiohttp.ClientSession, url: str, semaphore: asyncio.Semaphore) -> str:
    45	    if url == "":
    46	        return ""

    57	                raw = await response.read()

    65	async def fetch_all(urls: list[str], limit: int = 8) -> list[str]:
    66	    semaphore = asyncio.Semaphore(limit)
    67	    timeout = aiohttp.ClientTimeout(total=5)
    68	    connector = aiohttp.TCPConnector(limit_per_host=limit, force_close=True)
    69	
    70	    async with aiohttp.ClientSession(timeout=timeout, connector=connector) as session:
    71	        tasks = [fetch(session, url, semaphore) for url in urls]
    72	        return await asyncio.gather(*tasks)

    75	def collect_context(snippet: str, doc: str) -> str:
    76	    snippets = parse_snippet(snippet)
    77	    ctx_paras = []
    78	
    79	    for s in snippets:
    80	        pos = doc.replace("\n", " ").find(s)
    81	        if pos == -1:
    82	            continue
    83	        sta = pos
    84	        while sta > 0 and doc[sta] != "\n":
    85	            sta -= 1
    86	        end = pos + len(s)
    87	        while end < len(doc) and doc[end] != "\n":
    88	            end += 1
    89	        para = doc[sta:end].strip()
    90	        if para not in ctx_paras:
    91	            ctx_paras.append(para)
    92	
    93	    return "\n".join(ctx_paras)

    36	def _is_loopback_host(host: str | None) -> bool:

    76	class _GatewayState:
    77	    def __init__(self) -> None:

    90	    async def on_request_start(self) -> None:

    97	    async def on_request_end(self) -> None:
    98	        async with self.lock:
    99	            self.in_flight = max(0, self.in_flight - 1)

   101	    async def on_success(self, latency_ms: float) -> None:
   102	        async with self.lock:

   104	            self.success_latency_ms_sum += latency_ms
   105	
   106	    async def on_busy(self) -> None:

   152	def _extract_retrieve_results(payload: dict[str, Any]) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
   153	    result = payload.get("result", [])
   154	    if not isinstance(result, list) or not result:
   155	        return []
   156	    first = result[0]
   157	    if not isinstance(first, list):
   158	        return []
   159	    normalized: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
   160	    for item in first:
   161	        if isinstance(item, dict):
   162	            if "document" in item:
   163	                normalized.append(item)
   164	            else:
   165	                normalized.append({"document": item})
   166	    return normalized

   169	async def _ensure_session() -> aiohttp.ClientSession:
   170	    if STATE.session is None or STATE.session.closed:
   171	        connector = aiohttp.TCPConnector(limit=CONNECTOR_LIMIT, limit_per_host=CONNECTOR_PER_HOST)
   172	        timeout = aiohttp.ClientTimeout(total=None)
   173	        # Default to NOT inheriting process proxy env to keep local upstream (127.0.0.1) stable.
   174	        STATE.session = aiohttp.ClientSession(connector=connector, timeout=timeout, trust_env=TRUST_ENV)
   175	    return STATE.session

   178	async def _search_via_retrieval_server(
   179	    upstream_url: str,
   180	    query: str,
   181	    top_k: int,
   182	    timeout_sec: float,
   183	    proxy: str | None,
   184	) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
   185	    session = await _ensure_session()
   186	    payload = {"queries": [query], "topk": top_k, "return_scores": False}
   187	    effective_proxy = proxy
   188	    if BYPASS_PROXY_FOR_LOCAL_UPSTREAM and _is_loopback_url(upstream_url):
   189	        effective_proxy = None
   190	
   191	    total_attempts = max(1, RETRY_TIMES + 1)
   192	    last_err: Exception | None = None
   193	    for attempt in range(1, total_attempts + 1):
   194	        try:
   195	            timeout = aiohttp.ClientTimeout(total=timeout_sec)
   196	            async with session.post(upstream_url, json=payload, timeout=timeout, proxy=effective_proxy) as resp:
   197	                text = await resp.text()
   198	                if resp.status in (429, 500, 502, 503, 504):
   199	                    raise RuntimeError(f"upstream_http_{resp.status}: {text[:500]}")
   200	                resp.raise_for_status()
   201	                data = json.loads(text)
   202	                return _extract_retrieve_results(data)
   203	        except asyncio.TimeoutError as exc:
   204	            last_err = exc
   205	            if attempt < total_attempts:
   206	                await asyncio.sleep(RETRY_BACKOFF * attempt)
   207	                continue
   208	            raise
   209	        except Exception as exc:
   210	            last_err = exc
   211	            if attempt < total_attempts:
   212	                await asyncio.sleep(RETRY_BACKOFF * attempt)
   213	                continue
   214	            raise RuntimeError(str(last_err) if last_err is not None else "upstream_error")

   216	    raise RuntimeError("upstream_unreachable")

   219	async def local_search(
   220	    search_url: str,
   221	    query: str,
   222	    top_k: int = DEFAULT_TOPK,
   223	    timeout: float = UPSTREAM_TIMEOUT,
   224	    proxy: str | None = None,
   225	) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
   226	    if search_url.rstrip("/").endswith("/search"):
   227	        connector = aiohttp.TCPConnector(limit=CONNECTOR_LIMIT, limit_per_host=CONNECTOR_PER_HOST)
   228	        timeout_obj = aiohttp.ClientTimeout(total=timeout)
   229	        async with aiohttp.ClientSession(connector=connector, timeout=timeout_obj, trust_env=TRUST_ENV) as session:
   230	            payload = {"query": query, "top_k": top_k, "timeout": timeout, "proxy": proxy}
   231	            async with session.post(search_url, json=payload, proxy=proxy) as resp:
   232	                resp.raise_for_status()
   233	                data = await resp.json()
   234	                results = data.get("results", [])
   235	                if isinstance(results, list):
   236	                    return results
   237	                return []
   238	
   239	    return await _search_via_retrieval_server(
   240	        upstream_url=search_url,
   241	        query=query,
   242	        top_k=top_k,
   243	        timeout_sec=timeout,
   244	        proxy=proxy,

   269	@app.post("/search")
   270	async def search_endpoint(req: SearchRequest) -> dict[str, Any]:
   271	    query = req.query.strip()
   272	    if not query:
   273	        raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail="query is empty")
   274	
   275	    try:
   276	        await asyncio.wait_for(STATE.semaphore.acquire(), timeout=QUEUE_WAIT_TIMEOUT)
   277	    except asyncio.TimeoutError:
   278	        await STATE.on_busy()
   279	        raise HTTPException(status_code=429, detail="gateway busy: queue wait timeout exceeded")
   280	
   281	    await STATE.on_request_start()
   282	    started = time.perf_counter()
   283	    try:
   284	        results = await _search_via_retrieval_server(
   285	            upstream_url=UPSTREAM_URL,
   286	            query=query,
   287	            top_k=req.top_k,
   288	            timeout_sec=req.timeout,
   289	            proxy=req.proxy,
   290	        )
   291	        latency_ms = (time.perf_counter() - started) * 1000.0
   292	        await STATE.on_success(latency_ms)
   293	        return {
   294	            "status": "success",
   295	            "query": query,
   296	            "top_k": req.top_k,
   297	            "count": len(results),
   298	            "results": results,
   299	            "error": None,
   300	            "latency_ms": round(latency_ms, 3),
   301	        }
   302	    except asyncio.TimeoutError:
   303	        await STATE.on_timeout()
   304	        raise HTTPException(status_code=504, detail="upstream timeout")
   305	    except Exception as exc:
   306	        await STATE.on_upstream_error()
   307	        raise HTTPException(status_code=502, detail=f"upstream error: {exc}")
   308	    finally:
   309	        await STATE.on_request_end()
   310	        STATE.semaphore.release()

     1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
     2	
     3	import argparse
     4	from pathlib import Path
     5	
     6	import pandas as pd
     7	
     8	
     9	def parse_args():
    10	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Prepare a reproducible train/test split for Search-R1 parquet data.")
    11	    parser.add_argument("--input-parquet", type=str, required=True, help="Source parquet path.")
    12	    parser.add_argument("--output-train", type=str, required=True, help="Output train parquet path.")
    13	    parser.add_argument("--output-test", type=str, required=True, help="Output test parquet path.")
    14	    parser.add_argument("--test-ratio", type=float, default=0.03, help="Fraction used as test set.")
    15	    parser.add_argument("--seed", type=int, default=20260222, help="Random seed for shuffling/sampling.")
    16	    parser.add_argument(
    17	        "--max-rows",
    18	        type=int,
    19	        default=0,
    20	        help="If > 0, first sample this many rows from input before splitting (budget control).",
    21	    )
    22	    return parser.parse_args()
    23	
    24	
    25	def main():
    26	    args = parse_args()
    27	
    28	    input_path = Path(args.input_parquet)
    29	    out_train = Path(args.output_train)
    30	    out_test = Path(args.output_test)
    31	
    32	    if not input_path.exists():
    33	        raise FileNotFoundError(f"Input parquet not found: {input_path}")
    34	
    35	    if not (0.0 < args.test_ratio < 1.0):
    36	        raise ValueError(f"--test-ratio must be in (0,1), got {args.test_ratio}")
    37	
    38	    df = pd.read_parquet(input_path)
    39	    original_rows = len(df)
    40	
    41	    if args.max_rows and args.max_rows > 0:
    42	        sample_n = min(args.max_rows, original_rows)
    43	        df = df.sample(n=sample_n, random_state=args.seed, replace=False).reset_index(drop=True)
    44	
    45	    df = df.sample(frac=1.0, random_state=args.seed).reset_index(drop=True)
    46	
    47	    n_rows = len(df)
    48	    n_test = max(1, int(n_rows * args.test_ratio))
    49	    n_train = n_rows - n_test
    50	    if n_train <= 0:
    51	        raise ValueError(f"Train rows <= 0 after split: n_rows={n_rows}, n_test={n_test}")
    52	
    53	    test_df = df.iloc[:n_test].copy()
    54	    train_df = df.iloc[n_test:].copy()
    55	
    56	    out_train.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    57	    out_test.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    58	
    59	    train_df.to_parquet(out_train, index=False)
    60	    test_df.to_parquet(out_test, index=False)
    61	
    62	    print(
    63	        "Prepared split successfully: "
    64	        f"original_rows={original_rows}, used_rows={n_rows}, train_rows={len(train_df)}, test_rows={len(test_df)}, "
    65	        f"test_ratio={args.test_ratio}, seed={args.seed}, max_rows={args.max_rows}"
    66	    )
    67	    print(f"train={out_train}")
    68	    print(f"test={out_test}")
    69	
    70	
    71	if __name__ == "__main__":
    72	    main()

    14	def parse_args():
    15	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Export Search-R1 test trajectories from debug eval rollout data.")
    16	    parser.add_argument("--eval-rollout-pt", type=str, required=True, help="Path to eval_xxx.pt debug rollout file.")
    17	    parser.add_argument("--output-jsonl", type=str, required=True, help="Output jsonl path.")
    18	    parser.add_argument("--output-metrics-json", type=str, required=True, help="Output metrics json path.")
    19	    return parser.parse_args()
    20

    22	def _to_list(x):
    23	    if x is None:
    24	        return []
    25	    if isinstance(x, list):
    26	        return x
    27	    if hasattr(x, "tolist"):
    28	        value = x.tolist()
    29	        return value if isinstance(value, list) else [value]
    30	    return [x]
    31	
    32	
    33	def _extract_question(prompt):
    34	    text = ""
    35	    if isinstance(prompt, str):
    36	        text = prompt
    37	    elif isinstance(prompt, list):
    38	        parts = []
    39	        for msg in prompt:
    40	            if isinstance(msg, dict) and "content" in msg:
    41	                parts.append(str(msg["content"]))
    42	        text = "\n".join(parts)
    43	    else:
    44	        text = str(prompt)
    45	
    46	    m = re.search(r"Question:\s*(.*)", text, flags=re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
    47	    if m:
    48	        return m.group(1).strip()
    49	    return text.strip().splitlines()[-1] if text.strip() else ""
    50	
    51	
    52	def _safe_mean(values):
    53	    valid = [v for v in values if v is not None]
    54	    return mean(valid) if valid else None
    55	
    56	
    57	def main():
    58	    args = parse_args()
    59	
    60	    eval_path = Path(args.eval_rollout_pt)
    61	    out_jsonl = Path(args.output_jsonl)
    62	    out_metrics = Path(args.output_metrics_json)
    63	
    64	    if not eval_path.exists():
    65	        raise FileNotFoundError(f"Eval rollout file not found: {eval_path}")
    66	
    67	    payload = torch.load(eval_path, map_location="cpu", weights_only=False)
    68	    samples = payload.get("samples", [])
    69	
    70	    out_jsonl.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    71	    out_metrics.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    72	
    73	    rewards = []
    74	    accs = []
    75	    losses = []
    76	    entropies = []
    77	    response_lengths = []
    78	    search_calls = []
    79	
    80	    with out_jsonl.open("w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    81	        for sample in samples:
    82	            prompt = sample.get("prompt", "")
    83	            response = sample.get("response", "")
    84	            question = _extract_question(prompt)
    85	
    86	            reward = sample.get("reward")
    87	            if isinstance(reward, dict):
    88	                reward_value = reward.get("score", None)
    89	            else:
    90	                reward_value = reward
    91	
    92	            ground_truth = sample.get("label", {}).get("ground_truth", {}).get("target", [])
    93	            ground_truth = _to_list(ground_truth)
    94	
    95	            solution = f"{prompt}{response}"
    96	            pred_answer = extract_solution(solution)
    97	            acc = 0.0
    98	            if pred_answer is not None and ground_truth:
    99	                acc = float(em_check(pred_answer, ground_truth))
   100	
   101	            rollout_log_probs = sample.get("rollout_log_probs")
   102	            loss_mask = sample.get("loss_mask")
   103	            loss = None
   104	            entropy = None
   105	            if isinstance(rollout_log_probs, list) and isinstance(loss_mask, list) and len(rollout_log_probs) == len(loss_mask):
   106	                token_nll = [-float(lp) for lp, m in zip(rollout_log_probs, loss_mask, strict=True) if int(m) == 1]
   107	                if token_nll:
   108	                    loss = sum(token_nll) / len(token_nll)
   109	                    # Entropy is not directly emitted by rollout. Use token-level surprisal proxy for analysis.
   110	                    entropy = loss
   111	
   112	            record = {
   113	                "question": question,
   114	                "traj": response,
   115	                "reward": reward_value,
   116	                "acc": acc,
   117	                "loss": loss,
   118	                "entropy": entropy,
   119	                "response_length": sample.get("response_length"),
   120	                "effective_response_length": int(sum(loss_mask)) if isinstance(loss_mask, list) else sample.get("response_length"),
   121	                "search_calls": response.count("<search>"),
   122	                "status": sample.get("status"),
   123	                "ground_truth": ground_truth,
   124	                "pred_answer": pred_answer,
   125	            }
   126	            f.write(json.dumps(record, ensure_ascii=False) + "\n")
   127	
   128	            rewards.append(reward_value)
   129	            accs.append(acc)
   130	            losses.append(loss)
   131	            entropies.append(entropy)
   132	            response_lengths.append(sample.get("response_length"))
   133	            search_calls.append(record["search_calls"])
   134	
   135	    metrics = {
   136	        "num_samples": len(samples),
   137	        "reward_mean": _safe_mean(rewards),
   138	        "acc_mean": _safe_mean(accs),
   139	        "loss_mean": _safe_mean(losses),
   140	        "entropy_mean": _safe_mean(entropies),
   141	        "response_length_mean": _safe_mean(response_lengths),
   142	        "search_calls_mean": _safe_mean(search_calls),
   143	        "eval_rollout_id": payload.get("rollout_id"),
   144	        "eval_rollout_path": str(eval_path),
   145	        "note": "entropy_mean uses rollout token surprisal proxy because rollout API only returns sampled-token logprobs.",
   146	    }
   147	
   148	    out_metrics.write_text(json.dumps(metrics, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2), encoding="utf-8")
   149	
   150	    print(json.dumps(metrics, ensure_ascii=False))
   151	    print(f"Saved trajectories: {out_jsonl}")
   152	    print(f"Saved metrics: {out_metrics}")
   153	
   154

   156	    main()

     1	# Examples
     2	
     3	These examples provide concrete examples to leverage slime in your own RL workflow. Some examples are just demonstrative, but most of them are verifiable with a concrete performance score.
     4	
     5	## Directory Structure
     6	
     7	- **[compile-r1](./compile-r1)**: Search-R1-style code-generation rollout with a local Python sandbox tool on HumanEval.
     8	- **[eval_multi_task](./eval_multi_task)**: Example for supporting evaluation multiple tasks with different configs.
     9	- **[fully_async](./fully_async)**: Demonstrates fully asynchronous rollout generation for higher efficiency.
    10	- **[geo3k_vlm](./geo3k_vlm)**: Training VLMs with FSDP on a single-turn reasoning task using GRPO on the GEO3K dataset.
    11	- **[geo3k_vlm_multi_turn](./geo3k_vlm_multi_turn)**: VLM multi-turn training (FSDP backend) on Geo3k dataset.
    12	- **[low_precision](./low_precision)**: Examples of FP8 training and inference for improved throughput and stability.
    13	- **[multi_agent](./multi_agent)**: Example of running multi-agent RL with `slime`.
    14	- **[on_policy_distillation](./on_policy_distillation)**: Example implementation for on-policy distillation, extending the reinforcement learning pipeline to support teacherвҖ“student distillation directly within on-policy training.
    15	- **[reproducibility](./reproducibility)**: Guides on achieving bitwise experiment reproduction using deterministic modes.
    16	- **[retool](./retool)**: Demonstrates the retool functionality for tool-enabled language model generation.
    17	- **[search-r1](./search-r1)**: A minimal reproduction of Search-R1, featuring multi-turn conversation and tool-calling.
    18	- **[strands_sglang](./strands_sglang)**: Integration example with the Strands-Agents scaffolding framework.
    19	- **[tau-bench](./tau-bench)**: Training in an agentic multi-turn tool use environment (Tau-bench).
    20	- **[train_infer_mismatch_helper](./train_infer_mismatch_helper)**: Algorithmic methods for rollout correction (e.g., TIS, MIS).
    21	- **[true_on_policy](./true_on_policy)**: Ensures strictly equal log probabilities between inference (SGLang) and training engines.
    22	- **[true_on_policy_vlm](./true_on_policy_vlm)**: "True On-Policy" training demonstration for VLM (Qwen3-VL).

    18	def reset_arg(parser, name, **kwargs):
    19	    """
    20	    Reset the default value of a Megatron argument.
    21	    :param parser: The argument parser.
    22	    :param name: The name of the argument to reset.
    23	    :param default: The new default value.
    24	    """
    25	    for action in parser._actions:
    26	        if name in action.option_strings:
    27	            if "default" in kwargs:
    28	                action.default = kwargs["default"]
    29	            break
    30	    else:
    31	        parser.add_argument(name, **kwargs)

    22	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
